**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Melianse](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2230) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 71 **Perception** +11; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Aquan](/Languages.aspx?ID=14), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +11, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +12, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +11, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +13 **Str** +2 **Dex** +3 **Con** +1 **Int** +0 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +4 **Wild Empathy** Melianse can use [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) on [aquatic](/Traits.aspx?ID=168) or [amphibious](/Traits.aspx?ID=207) [animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=9) and make very simple [Requests](/Actions.aspx?ID=51) of them.
 --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +13 **Will** +11 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) **HP** 78 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 5 --- **Speed** 20 feet, swim 30 feet **Melee** claw +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 2d6+5 slashing **Primal Spontaneous Spells** DC 22, attack +14 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Dancing Lights](/Spells.aspx?ID=58), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [Ray of Frost](/Spells.aspx?ID=245), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280), [Tanglefoot](/Spells.aspx?ID=330) - **1st** [Goblin Pox](/Spells.aspx?ID=139), [Grease](/Spells.aspx?ID=140), [Spider Sting](/Spells.aspx?ID=300), [Ventriloquism](/Spells.aspx?ID=356) (4 slots) - **2nd** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Glitterdust](/Spells.aspx?ID=136), [Obscuring Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=210), [Web](/Spells.aspx?ID=374) (4 slots) - **3rd** [Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=94), [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=147), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Wall of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=367) (4 slots) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 22, attack +14 - **1st** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) (×3), [Hydraulic Push](/Spells.aspx?ID=154) - **2nd** [Water Breathing](/Spells.aspx?ID=370) **Rituals** - **2nd** [Inveigle](/Rituals.aspx?ID=14) **Grant Desire** ([Divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Melianse can duplicate any 3rd-level spell or produce any effect with a power level in line with a 3rd-level spell, but only in response to the request or desire of a non-[fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69) creature. The creature whose desire is granted can never again benefit from Melianse's Grant Desire ability
name: Melianse

**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Hateful Hermit](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2231) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 74 **Perception** +11 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +13, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +12, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +11 **Str** +4 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** +1 **Wis** +0 **Cha** +1 **Items** [Blowgun](/Weapons.aspx?ID=66) (10 darts), [Graveroot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=116) (4 doses), [Hide Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=7), [Sickle](/Weapons.aspx?ID=9), _+1 [whip](/Weapons.aspx?ID=48)_ --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +11 **Will** +9 +2 status to all saves vs. [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effects **HP** 80 **Resistances** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) 5 --- **Speed** 25 feet; trackless step **Melee** _whip_ +14 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [Reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 1d4+7 slashing **Melee** sickle +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 1d4+7 slashing **Ranged** blowgun +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188)), **Damage** 1 piercing plus graveroot **Focus Hatred** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The hateful hermit unleashes a vexing stream of profanities and threats at a single foe he can see within 30 feet. The hermit gains a +1 circumstance bonus on all attack rolls made against that target until he chooses a different target to Focus Hatred on. The target must attempt a DC 22 Will save, with the below effects. The hateful hermit cannot Focus Hatred again for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Focus Hatred for 24 hours.
**Success** The target is distracted by the hatred and is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against the hermit's next attack.
**Failure** The hermit's words unnerve the target and distract them; the target is flat-footed until the end of the hateful hermit's next turn.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target is flat-footed for 1 minute. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** The hateful hermit deals 2d6 extra precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures. **Trackless Step** The hateful hermit always gains the benefits of [Cover Tracks](/Actions.aspx?ID=65) in natural surroundings, even when moving at full speed.
name: Hateful Hermit

**Clench Jaws** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** Old Crackjaw damaged a creature with a jaws Strike with its previous action; **Effect** Old Crackjaw clenches its jaw and automatically deals an additional 2d8 bludgeoning damage to the creature it just hit.

**Gnawing Bite** <actions string="Two Actions" />  Old Crackjaw makes a jaws Strike against an object. This counts as two attacks when calculating Old Crackjaw's multiple attack penalty. If this Strike hits, Old Crackjaw deals an extra die of weapon damage to the object.

**Shell Defense** <actions string="Single Action" />  Old Crackjaw retracts its limbs and head into its shell and ends its turn. This increases its AC to 25, but it can't act except to reemerge, which it can do as a single action.

</column>

swamp stride **Melee** _whip_ +18 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [Reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 2d6+8 slashing **Melee** tongue +16 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** tongue grab **Ranged** blowgun +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188)), **Damage** 1 piercing plus [giant wasp venom](/Equipment.aspx?ID=115) **Divine Prepared Spells** DC 26, attack +18 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=84), [Know Direction](/Spells.aspx?ID=169), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280), [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=307) - **1st** [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=45), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244) - **2nd** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [See Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=271) - **3rd** [Blindness](/Spells.aspx?ID=26), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Vampiric Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=354) - **4th** [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=86), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Sound Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=292) **Gogunta's Croak** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The boggard cultist unleashes a terrifying croak. Any non-boggard within 30 feet must attempt a DC 25 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is temporarily immune to
Gogunta's Croak for 1 minute. **Success** The creature is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19)
**Failure** The creature is frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is frightened 3 and is also [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=17) during their next turn. **Swamp Stride** A boggard cultist ignores difficult terrain caused by swamp terrain features. **Tongue Grab** If the boggard cultist hits a creature with its tongue, that creature becomes [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by the boggard. Unlike with a normal Grab, the creature isn't [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24), but it can't move beyond the reach of the boggard's tongue. A creature can sever the tongue by hitting AC 25 and dealing at least 10 slashing damage. Though this doesn't deal any damage to the boggard, it prevents it from using its tongue Strike until it regrows its tongue, which takes a week.
name: Boggard Cultist

**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 41 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 [Irahkatu](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2238) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 96 **Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +25, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +23, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +25 **Str** +8 **Dex** +6 **Con** +6 **Int** +0 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 **Voice Imitation** Irahkatu can mimic the sounds of a person in distress by attempting a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) check to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47). Irahkatu has a +4 circumstance bonus to this check. --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +21 **Will** +20 **HP** 235 --- **Speed** 25 feet, swim 35 feet **Melee** jaws +27, **Damage** 3d12+14 piercing **Melee** claw +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 3d8+14 slashing **Melee** tail claw +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d6+14 slashing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Disfigure** **Requirements** Irahkatu has a Medium or smaller creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) with his tail claw or has already damaged a creature with his claw or tail claw Strike this turn; **Effect** Irahkatu attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the creature's Fortitude DC.
**Critical Success** Irahkatu tears at the target's eyes. The target takes 14d6 slashing damage and is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 24 hours. This blinded condition is removed earlier if the creature is fully healed of all damage.
**Success** Irahkatu mangles some fingers. The target takes 7d6 slashing damage and is [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) for 24 hours.
**Failure** Irahkatu claws at the creature, dealing 3d6 slashing damage.
**Critical Failure** The creature is unaffected. **Tail Drag** **Requirements** Irahkatu has a Medium or smaller creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) with his tail claw; **Effect** Irahkatu attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the creature's Fortitude DC.
**Critical Success** If the creature is 10 feet away from Irahkatu, it is [dragged](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) into a square adjacent to it. Irahkatu can make a jaws Strike against the creature.
**Success** If the creature is 10 feet away from Irahkatu, it is dragged into a square adjacent to Irahkatu.
**Failure** The creature is not dragged.
**Critical Failure** The creature is not dragged and Irahkatu no longer has the creature grabbed.
name: Irahkatu

**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 42 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 [Hooktongue](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2239) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 97 **Perception** +25; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Aquan](/Languages.aspx?ID=14); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +28, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +27 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +5 **Int** -3 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +0 **Slow Metabolism** Hooktongue can go for 10 years without feeding. After this, its hunger makes it [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) but doesn't otherwise impact its lifespan. If Hooktongue is slowed as a result of starvation, it can remove this condition by using Swallow Whole to gulp down a meal.
**Undetectable** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) Hooktongue automatically tries to counteract any [detection](/Traits.aspx?ID=43), [revelation](/Traits.aspx?ID=138), or [scrying](/Traits.aspx?ID=141) [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47) attempted against it, using its [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) modifier for the [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check. --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +27 **Ref** +25 **Will** +23 **HP** 275 **Resistances** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 15, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 15 --- **Speed** 20 feet, swim 50 feet **Melee** jaws +30 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d12+14 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** tongue +30 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+14 piercing plus Hooktongue Venom **Capsize** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) Hooktongue attempts to capsize an aquatic vessel of its size or smaller that it's adjacent to. It must succeed at an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check with a DC of 35 or the pilot's [Sailing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) DC, whichever is higher. **Hooktongue Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** Fortitude DC 34; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d6 poison damage and [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) (1 round); **Stage 2** 3d6 poison damage, clumsy 1, and [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) (1 round); **Stage 3** 4d6 poison damage, clumsy 2, and confused (1 round) **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35)** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) Large, 2d10+10 bludgeoning, Rupture 28 **Poison Spray** ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) Hooktongue sprays poison from its throat in an 80-foot line that deals 15d6 poison damage (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). A creature that fails or critically fails this save is exposed to Hooktongue Venom as well. Hooktongue can't use Poison Spray again for 1d4 rounds. **Water Travel** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) Hooktongue dissolves into water, appearing only as a long, serpentine silhouette on the waves. While in this form, Hooktongue's swim Speed increases to 600 feet, it automatically succeeds at [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to Swim, and it gains a +4 circumstance bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks in water. Hooktongue can remain in this form for 8 hours but can't enter salt water when using this ability. Hooktongue can return to its normal form using a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait.
name: Hooktongue

**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Bog Mummy Cultist](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2242) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 102 **Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +19, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +18 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +3 **Items** _+1 [cold-resistant](/Equipment.aspx?ID=283) [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4)_ --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +16 **Will** +21 **HP** 135 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** cold 5, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 10 **Weaknesses** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 10 **Breath of the Bog** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 30 feet. A creature that begins its turn within the area feels as if its lungs were filling with water and must succeed at a DC 25 Fortitude save or be unable to speak or [breathe](/Rules.aspx?ID=468). The creature can hold its breath and can attempt a new save at the end of its turn. A creature that succeeds is temporarily immune to breath of the bog for 24 hours.
**Rejuvenation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) Unless the damage that destroys a bog mummy cultist is positive damage, necromantic energies rebuild its body from swamp muck 1 week later. If the uncompleted body is destroyed during that time, the process starts anew. Anyone who succeeds at a DC 26 [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) realizes that a [_consecrate_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=8) ritual performed on the site that a bog mummy cultist was destroyed prevents its rejuvenation, the way positive damage prevents the rejuvenation from occurring.
**Rise Up** **Trigger** A creature walks on top of a bog mummy cultist that lies buried in the mud or peat below; **Requirements** Initiative has not yet been rolled; **Effect** The bog mummy automatically notices the creature and [Burrows](/Actions.aspx?ID=93) before rolling initiative. --- **Speed** 20 feet, burrow 15 feet **Melee** fist +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d8+10 plus bog rot **Divine Innate Spells** DC 28, attack +20 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=84) - **4th** [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=86), [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=141) - **5th** [Crisis of Faith](/Spells.aspx?ID=55) **Bog Rot** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118)) This affliction can't be reduced below stage 1, nor can the damage from it be healed, until it's successfully treated with [_remove curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=250) or a similar effect; the affliction can then be removed as normal for a disease. A creature killed by bog rot melts into a noxious sludge and can't be brought back to life except by a 7th-level [_resurrect_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=20) ritual or similar magic; **Saving Throw** DC 28 Fortitude; **Stage 1** carrier with no ill effect (1 minute); **Stage 2** 3d6 negative damage and [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) (1 day)
name: Bog Mummy Cultist

**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 41 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 [Hooktongue Hydra](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2243) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 103 **Perception** +26; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +27, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +24 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +6 **Int** -3 **Wis** +5 **Cha** -1 --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +24 **Will** +22 all-around vision **HP** 240 ((body), hydra regeneration 15
**HP** 45 (head), head regrowth) **Immunities** area damage **Weaknesses** slashing 5 **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**Head Regrowth** The Hooktongue Hydra has twelve heads. A creature can attempt to sever one of the hydra's heads by specifically targeting it and dealing damage equal to the head's Hit Points. A head that is not completely severed returns to full Hit Points at the end of any creature's turn. A hydra can regrow a severed head using Hydra Regeneration. A creature can prevent this regrowth by dealing acid or fire damage to the stump, cauterizing it. Single-target [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3) or [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) effects need to be targeted at a specific stump, but effects that deal [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage or affect areas covering the hydra's whole space cauterize all stumps if they deal acid or fire damage. If the attack that severs a head deals any acid or fire damage, the stump is cauterized instantly. If all five heads are cauterized, the hydra dies.
**Hydra Regeneration** The hydra has [regeneration](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=26) equal to 3 x the number of heads it has. If a hydra's body is missing any heads and the remaining stumps have not been cauterized, the hydra attempts a DC 35 Fortitude save after it regains Hit Points from regeneration. On a success, one uncauterized stump regrows two heads; on a critical success, two uncauterized stumps regrow into two heads each. The hydra can never grow more than double the number of heads it ordinarily has. The hydra's regeneration only fully deactivates if all its heads are severed and all stumps are cauterized, at which point it dies.
**Multiple Opportunities** A hydra gains an extra reaction per round for each of its heads beyond the first, which it can use only to make Attacks of Opportunity. It can't use more than 1 reaction on the same triggering action, even if a creature leaves several squares within its reach, and the hydra must use a different head for each Attack of Opportunity it makes. Whenever one of the hydra's heads is severed, the hydra loses 1 of its extra reactions per round. --- **Speed** 25 feet, swim 25 feet **Melee** fangs +27 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+14 piercing **Focused Assault** The hydra attacks a single target with its heads, overwhelming its foe with multiple attacks and leaving almost nowhere to dodge. The hydra Strikes with its fangs. On a successful attack, the hydra deals damage from its fangs Strike to the target, plus an additional 1d6 damage for every head it has beyond the first. Even on a failed attack, the hydra deals the damage from one fangs Strike to the target creature, though it still misses completely on a critical failure. This counts toward the hydra's multiple attack penalty as a number of attacks equal to the number of heads the hydra has. **Storm of Jaws** The hydra makes a number of Strikes up to its number of heads, each against a different target. These attacks count toward the hydra's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after the hydra makes all its attacks.
name: Hooktongue Hydra

Losing a head doesn't kill a chimera (as long as it has one head left), but it does prevent it from making Strikes with the lost head or using the head's Breath Weapon.
**Triple Opportunity** An oversized chimera gains 2 extra reactions each round that it can use only to make Attacks of Opportunity. It must use a different head for each reaction, and it can't use more than one on the same triggering action. If it loses one of its heads, it also loses one of these extra reactions.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** dragon jaws +26, **Damage** 2d6+11 piercing plus 3d6 fire **Melee** lion jaws +26, **Damage** 3d10+11 piercing **Melee** goat horns +26, **Damage** 3d10+11 piercing **Melee** claw +26 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 3d8+11 slashing **Breath Weapon** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) The oversized chimera breathes out a blistering cone of fire that deals 13d6 fire damage to all creatures in the area (DC 32 basic Reflex save). **Draconic Bite** An oversized chimera's dragon head deals an extra 3d6 fire damage. **Three-Headed Strike** The oversized chimera makes a Strike with its dragon jaws, lion jaws, and goat horns, each at a –2 penalty and targeting a different creature. These Strikes count as only 1 attack for the chimera's multiple attack penalty, and the penalty doesn't increase until after it has made all 3 attacks.
name: Oversized Chimera

The hill giant butcher gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to Seek the prey and to Intimidation checks to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) the prey. This effect lasts until the hill giant butcher uses Menace Prey again. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** The hill giant butcher deals an extra 2d6 precision damage to flat-footed creatures. **Throw Rock** **Twin Butchery** **Frequency** once per round; **Requirement** the hill giant butcher is wielding two melee weapons, each in a different hand; **Effect** The hill giant butcher uses both of its axes against a single target. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Ilora Nuski](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2263) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 112 **Perception** +22 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Hallit](/Languages.aspx?ID=37), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +23, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +21, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +22, [River Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +22, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +25, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +24 **Str** +3 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** -1 **Items** [Boots of Bounding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=412), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [shortswords](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43)_ (2), _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [studded leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=5)_ --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +25 **Will** +22 **HP** 220 **Twin Riposte** **Trigger** A creature within Ilora's reach critically fails a melee Strike against her** **Requirements** Ilora is benefiting from Twin Parry; **Effect** Ilora attempts a melee Strike or uses a [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) action against the triggering opponent.
 --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** shortsword +26 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d6+6 piercing **Primal Prepared Spells** DC 30, attack +22 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Electric Arc](/Spells.aspx?ID=97), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=171), [Produce Flame](/Spells.aspx?ID=236), [Tanglefoot](/Spells.aspx?ID=330) - **1st** [Alarm](/Spells.aspx?ID=7), [Pass Without Trace](/Spells.aspx?ID=215) - **2nd** [Animal Messenger](/Spells.aspx?ID=11), [Entangle](/Spells.aspx?ID=103) - **3rd** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) **Nature's Edge** Ilora always finds the weak points in her foes' defenses when they're on unfavorable terrain. Enemies are [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to her if they're in difficult terrain, restrained, or [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) due to natural or magical environmental effects. **Twin Parry** **Requirements** Ilora wields two melee weapons, one in each hand; **Effect** Ilora gains a +1 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of her next turn. She loses this bonus if she no longer meets the requirements. **Twin Takedown** **Frequency** once per round; **Requirements** Ilora wields two melee weapons, each in a different hand. **Effect** Ilora makes two Strikes against her target, one with each of the required weapons. If both hit the same target, combine the damage from the weapons for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses. She applies her multiple attack penalty to each Strike normally. **Wild Stride** Ilora moves quickly through obstacles. She ignores the effects of nonmagical difficult terrain and treats the hindrances of greater difficult terrain as those of difficult terrain. Within 300 feet of a river bank, she ignores the effects of all nonmagical difficult and greater difficult terrain.
name: Ilora Nuski

It automatically takes 43 as the result of its Deception checks and has a Deception DC of 43 to pass as a dead tree. **Woodland Ambush** **Requirements** The Tree that Weeps is using Dead Tree in forested terrain, and a creature that hasn't detected it is within 30 feet; **Effect** The Tree that Weeps Strides with a +10-foot circumstance bonus to its Speed in the direction of the triggering creature. Once the creature is in reach, the Tree that Weeps makes a scythe branch Strike against it. The creature is flat-footed against this Strike.

**Savage** **Trigger** A creature grabbed by Minognos-Ushad critically fails a check to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79)** **Effect** Minognos-Ushad makes a stinger Strike against the triggering creature. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 100 feet **Melee** fangs +37 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d10+18 piercing **Melee** claw +37 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d8+18 slashing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Melee** stinger +37 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d6+18 piercing plus wyvern venom **Breath Weapon** ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) Minognos-Ushad breathes a 60-foot cone of poison. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 25 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 [Dingetooth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2267) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 121 **Perception** +5; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +6, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +2, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +6, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +6 **Str** +0 **Dex** +3 **Con** +0 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** -1 **Self-Loathing** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) A mitflit's self-loathing makes it easy to influence. It takes a –4 penalty to its Will DC against checks to [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=52), [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53), [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50), and [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=51).
**Vermin Empathy** Mitflits can use Diplomacy to Make an Impression on and Request things of arthropods (insects, spiders, scorpions, crabs, and similar invertebrate animals). Most arthropods have a starting attitude of indifferent to mitflits. **Items** [Marbles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1336) (6), [Shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43) --- **AC** 16 **Fort** +3 **Ref** +8 **Will** +5 **HP** 25 **Weaknesses** cold iron 2 --- **Speed** 20 feet; climb 20 feet **Melee** shortsword +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 1d6 piercing **Primal Innate Spells** DC 17 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=229) - **1st** [Bane](/Spells.aspx?ID=18) - **2nd** [Speak with Animals](/Spells.aspx?ID=293) (at will; arthropods only) **Launch Marbles** The mitflit uses the marble launcher to fire six marbles at a creature in area A1. The target must attempt a DC 16 Reflex save.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is distracted by the marbles and is [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) 2 until the end of its next turn.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but one of the marbles hits the creature somewhere sensitive, causing 1d6 nonlethal bludgeoning damage. **Vengeful Anger** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) As long as it isn't [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), a mitflit gains a +2 status bonus to damage rolls against a creature that has previously damaged or tormented it.
name: Dingetooth

**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [The Lonely Warrior](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2270) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 135 **Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Hallit](/Languages.aspx?ID=37), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +15, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +14, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +13 **Str** +6 **Dex** +2 **Con** +6 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +3 **Items** [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2) _+1 [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=272) [flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295) [bastard sword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=15)_, [Chain Mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=9), [Wooden Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=2) (Hardness 3, HP 12, BT 6) --- **AC** 23 (25 with shield raised) **Fort** +15 **Ref** +13 **Will** +14 **HP** 106 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** death effects, disease, paralyze, poison, unconscious **Final Spite** **Trigger** The Lonely Warrior is reduced to 0 Hit Points** **Effect** The Lonely Warrior makes a Strike before being destroyed. It doesn't gain any temporary Hit Points from its drain life on this Strike. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _bastard sword_ +18, **Damage** 1d8+7 slashing plus drain life **Cairn Wight Spawn** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) A living humanoid slain by the Lonely Warrior's weapon or claw Strike rises as a spawned wight after 1d4 rounds. This spawned wight is under the command of the Lonely Warrior. It doesn't have drain life or cairn wight spawn and is [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) 2 for as long as it is a spawned wight. If its creator dies, the spawned wight becomes a full-fledged, autonomous cairn wight; it regains its free will, gains drain life and cairn wight spawn, and is no longer clumsy. **Drain Life** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When the Lonely Warrior damages a living creature with a melee Strike, using an unarmed attack or its bound weapon, it gains 5 temporary Hit Points, and the creature must succeed at a DC 21 Fortitude save or become [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) 1. Further damage dealt by it increases the drained condition value by 1 on a failed save, to a maximum of drained 4. **Lonely Dirge** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) The Lonely Warrior's funeral dirge is somewhat different from the standard cairn wight dirge: its droning, unnerving chant forces living creatures within 50 feet to attempt a DC 24 Will save. The Lonely Warrior can't chant a new Lonely Dirge for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) 1 by gnawing pangs of loneliness until the end of its next turn.
**Failure** The target is stupefied 1 for 1 hour.
**Critical Failure** The target is stupefied 1 for 24 hours and takes a –2 status penalty to saves against drain life.
name: The Lonely Warrior

**Magic Immunity** A will-o'-wisp is immune to all spells except [_faerie fire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=107), [_glitterdust_](/Spells.aspx?ID=136), [_magic missile_](/Spells.aspx?ID=180), and [_maze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=187). --- **Speed** fly 50 feet **Melee** shock +19 ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d8+6 electricity **Occult Innate Spells** DC 25 - **3rd** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=194), [Nightmare](/Spells.aspx?ID=208) - **4th** [Modify Memory](/Spells.aspx?ID=200) **Feed on Fear** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Requirement** An enemy is under a fear effect or dying within 15 feet of the will-o'-wisp. **Effect** The will-o'wisp feeds on the creature's terror. It regains 2d4 Hit Points, and if it has Gone Dark, its glow reignites. A will-o'-wisp can take this action only once per round. **Go Dark** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) The will-o'-wisp extinguishes its glow, becoming invisible. It can end this effect with another use of this action. If it uses its shock attack while invisible, the arc of electricity lets any observer determine its location, making the will-o'-wisp only hidden to all observers until it moves. **Invoke Stisshak** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The Spirit of Stisshak manifests a shimmering form, that of a regal lizardfolk king made of flickering ghostly light. This image is Medium and must manifest within 10 feet of the will-o'-wisp. The will-o'-wisp can sustain the image by concentrating, and can speak through it and control minor motions (such as movement of limbs), but cannot cause it to leave the square in which it appeared. The will-o'-wisp gains a +2 circumstance bonus on Intimidation checks made to [demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) foes through this invoked image. Any damage inflicted to the will-o'-wisp or the illusion itself causes the invoked illusion to vanish immediately.
name: Spirit Of Stisshak

**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 33 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 [Rigg Gargadilly](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2274) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 150 **Perception** +15; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +17, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +13, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +15, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +17, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +15, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +15 **Str** +0 **Dex** +6 **Con** +2 **Int** +4 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +4 **Items** [Hunting Spider Venom](/Equipment.aspx?ID=118) (3 doses), [_+1 shortsword_](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43) --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +13 **Ref** +17 **Will** +15 **HP** 70 **Weaknesses** cold iron 5 **Slow Susceptibility** The quickling takes a –2 status penalty to saving throws against effects that cause the [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) condition. If the quickling ever becomes slowed, they lose their supernatural speed, can't Fade from View, and become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) for the duration of the slow. They also can't reduce this sickened condition for the duration of the slowed effect.
**Can't Catch Me** **Trigger** The quickling is targeted by a Strike** **Effect** The quickling darts aside, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to AC, then Strides up to half their Speed after the Strike resolves. --- **Speed** 100 feet **Melee** _shortsword_ +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 1d6+3 piercing plus hunting spider venom **Primal Innate Spells** DC 20 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Dancing Lights](/Spells.aspx?ID=58), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=229) - **2nd** [Ventriloquism](/Spells.aspx?ID=356) - **4th** [Shatter](/Spells.aspx?ID=279) **Fade from View** **Requirements** The quickling used no [attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), or [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) actions in the previous round; **Effect** The quickling becomes [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) until it uses an attack, manipulate, or move action. The quickling can't use Fade from View again for 1d4 rounds. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** The quickling's Strikes deal an extra 3d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures. **Supernatural Speed** The quickling's speed, combined with nearly instantaneous acceleration and deceleration, enables them to move in astonishing ways. As long as they have a firm surface to travel across, they can Stride their full movement vertically or horizontally. They can even run across unstable surfaces, such as water, in the same way, although dangerous surfaces (acid, lava, etc.) harm them as normal. They must end their movement on a horizontal surface capable of bearing their weight or else they fall.
name: Rigg Gargadilly

**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [The Dancing Lady](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2275) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 153 **Perception** +16; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +18, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +18, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +15, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +16 **Str** +4 **Dex** +6 **Con** +3 **Int** +3 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +17 **Will** +14 **HP** 135 **Weaknesses** cold iron 10 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** claw +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 2d6+7 slashing and 1d6 persistent bleed **Primal Innate Spells** DC 26 - **2nd** [Entangle](/Spells.aspx?ID=103) - **3rd** [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=194) (at will) - **4th** [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) **Captivating Dance** ([Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) The baobhan sith sways rhythmically and Strides up to their Speed. Each creature within 30 feet of the baobhan sith at the end of their movement must attempt a DC 26 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Captivating Dance for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 round and is temporarily immune to Captivating Dance for 24 hours.
**Failure** The creature is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14), and it must spend at least 1 of its actions on each of its turns to move closer to the baobhan sith as expediently as possible, while avoiding obvious dangers. If the creature ends its movement or turn adjacent to the fey, it is slowed 1 until the end of its next turn.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature must spend each of its actions moving closer to the baobhan sith, and if it ends its movement or turn adjacent to the fey, it is [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) until the end of its next turn. **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The baobhan sith can transform into a large raven, with the effects from the bird option in the [_aerial form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=4) spell. **Drink Blood** **Requirements** A bleeding creature is within the baobhan sith's reach; **Effect** The baobhan sith tries to Drink the Blood from the bleeding creature's wounds. The baobhan sith attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the victim's Fortitude DC. On a success, the victim becomes [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) and the baobhan sith regains 10 HP, gaining any Hit Points in excess of their maximum Hit Points as temporary Hit Points. Drinking Blood from a victim that's already drained doesn't restore any Hit Points to the baobhan sith but increases the victim's drained value by 1. A victim's drained condition decreases by 1 per week. A blood transfusion, which requires 10 minutes, a successful DC 20 [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) check, and sufficient blood or a blood donor, reduces the drained value by 1.
name: The Dancing Lady

**Nature's Edge**   Boggard wardens are trained to take advantage of their foes' weak points when they're on unfavorable terrain. Enemies are [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to the boggard warden if they're in natural difficult terrain.

**[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)**   A boggard warden deals 2d6 extra precision damage to flat-footed creatures.

**Swamp Stride**   A boggard warrior ignores difficult terrain caused by swamp terrain features.

**Terrifying Croak** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The boggard warrior unleashes a terrifying croak. Any non-boggard within 30 feet becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) 1 unless they succeed at a DC 28 Will save; those who critically succeed are temporarily immune for 1 minute.

**Tongue Grab**   If the boggard warrior hits a creature with its tongue, that creature becomes [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by the boggard. Unlike with a normal grab, the creature isn't [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24), but it can't move beyond the reach of the boggard's tongue. A creature can sever the tongue by hitting AC 24 and dealing at least 10 slashing damage. Though this doesn't deal any damage to the boggard, it prevents it from using its tongue Strike until it regrows its tongue, which takes a week.

</column>
name: Boggard Warden

**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Akiros Ismort](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2284) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 180 **Perception** +10 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +11, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +9, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +7, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +7 **Str** +4 **Dex** +1 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +0 **Cha** +2 healing potions (2), _+1 longsword_, steel shield, rusted iron holy symbol of Erastil, silver Stag Lord amulet (worth 3 gp), 8 gp **Items** [chainmail](/Equipment.aspx?ID=11), [Composite Longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=74) (20 arrows), [_lesser healing potions_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=186) (2), [_+1 longsword_](/Weapons.aspx?ID=31), [Steel Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=3), [rusted iron holy symbol of Erastil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=41), silver Stag Lord amulet (worth 3 gp), 8 gp --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +8 **Will** +7 **HP** 53 **Shameful Memories** ([ <%TRAITS%60%%> emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) <%END>, [ <%TRAITS%106%%> mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) <%END>, [ <%TRAITS%163%%> visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) If Akiros sees another creature openly wearing Erastil's holy symbol <%END>, he must attempt a DC 15 Will save. Unless he critically fails his saving throw, he is temporarily immune to Shameful Memories for 24 hours.
**Critical Success** Akiros is unaffected.
**Success** Akiros is distracted by shame and becomes [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) 1 for 1 round.
**Failure** Akiros becomes sickened 2 with his shame.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but Akiros is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) 1 as well. He does not become temporarily immune to Shameful Memories, and he can be affected by this weakness again) this likely means he will have to immediately attempt the save again once he recovers from the stun. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _longsword_ +12 ([versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 1d8+6 slashing **Ranged** composite longbow +8 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=191), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=201)), **Damage** 1d8+2 piercing **No Escape** **Trigger** An adjacent foe moves away. **Effect** Akiros can Stride up to his speed, following the triggering creature and keeping it in reach throughout its movement until it stops moving or Akiros has moved his full Speed. **Rage** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) Akiros flies into a rage. He gains 6 temporary Hit Points, deals 2 additional damage with melee Strikes, takes a –1 penalty to AC, and can't use other actions with the concentrate trait. This effect lasts for 1 minute, until he falls unconscious, oruntil there are no enemies he can perceive, whichever comes first, at which point he loses any remaining temporary Hit Points from Rage and can't Rage again for 1 minute.
name: Akiros Ismort

He immediately heals 1d6 Hit Points per level of the slain animal, gaining any excess healing as temporary Hit Points that last for 10 minutes. As long as he possesses these temporary Hit Points, blood-red energy ripples across his flesh and his AC increases to 22.

**Swift Summon** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Prerequisite** Nugrah has a summon animal spell prepared and available. **Effect** Nugrah casts summon animal but does not need to use a material component.

</column>

**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 33 **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 33 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 [Kundal](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2286) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 190 **Perception** +15; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Hallit](/Languages.aspx?ID=37); wolf empathy **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +17, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +11, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +15, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +15 **Str** +4 **Dex** +2 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +0 **Wolf Empathy** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) Kundal can communicate with lupines. --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +13 **Will** +17 **HP** 140 **Weaknesses** silver 10 **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** 35 feet **Melee** jaws +17, **Damage** 2d12+9 piercing plus curse of the werewolf **Melee** claw +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d8+7 slashing **Ambush Strike** **Requirement** Kundal rolled Stealth for Initiative, and it's the first round of combat. **Effect** Kundal Strides up to his speed and then attempts a jaws Strike and a claw Strike against a single adjacent target. Apply his multiple attack penalty to each Strike normally. **Change Shape** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) Human with fist +16 for 1d4+7 bludgeoning, or wolf with Speed 40 feet and jaws with [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=20). **Curse of the Werewolf** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) This curse affects only humanoids. Saving Throw DC 25 Fortitude save. On each full moon, the cursed creature must succeed at another Fortitude save or turn into a werewolf until dawn. The creature is under the GM's control and goes on a rampage for half the night before falling unconscious until dawn. **Moon Frenzy** ([Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) Kundal's moon frenzy functions a bit differently than for typical werecreatures: he doesn't become Large sized, but he does grow much more swift and powerful. His speed increases to 35 feet during moon frenzy and increases the damage of his jaws by +2 (these adjustments are calculated into the stats above). In addition, while in moon frenzy, at the start of his turn, Kundal gains an additional reaction that can be used only to make an Attack of Opportunity. **Rip and Drag** **Requirement** Kundal damaged a target with his jaws Strike with a previous action this round. **Effect** Kundal chews the bitten creature as he attempts to overwhelm them by attempting an Athletics check against the bitten creature's Fortitude DC.
**Critical Success** Kundal inflicts 5d6 piercing damage on the target, exposes them to the curse of the werewolf, and can Stride up to half his speed. If his target is Medium or smaller, he drags the target with him into a new adjacent square.
**Success Kundal** inflicts 5d6 piercing damage on the target and exposes them to the curse of the werewolf. He can reposition the target up to five feet into an adjacent square.
**Failure** As success, except only 2d6 piercing damage.
**Critical Failure** Kundal fails to inflict any damage.
name: Kundal

**Site Bound** A typical ghost can stray only a short distance from where it was killed or the place it haunts. A typical limit is 120 feet. Some ghosts are instead bound to a room, building, item, or creature that was special to it rather than a location.

</column>

</row>

death effects, disease, paralyzed, poison, precision, unconscious

all damage 10 (except force, _ghost touch_, or positive; double resistance vs. non-magical)

**Rejuvenation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) Jin is put to rest if both Darivan (area C7) and the bloom of Lamashtu (area C10) are defeated.

</column>

---

**Speed** fly 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
ghostly hand +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),

- **6th**
[Awaken Animal](/Rituals.aspx?ID=3), [Commune with Nature](/Rituals.aspx?ID=7), [Primal Call](/Rituals.aspx?ID=19)

**Frightful  Moan** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The ghost laments its fate, forcing each living creature within 30 feet to attempt a Will save. On a failure, a creature becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) 2 (or frightened 3 on a critical failure). On a success, a creature is temporarily immune to this ghost's frightful moan for 1 minute.

**Telekinetic  Assault** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) The ghost cries out in pain and anguish as small objects and debris fly about in a 30-foot emanation. Creatures in this area take 5d6 bludgeoning damage, subject to a basic Reflex save.

</column>
name: Jin Durwhimmer

**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Akuzhail](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2298) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 247 **Perception** +21; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +19, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +21 **Str** +7 **Dex** +3 **Con** +5 **Int** -3 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +17 **Will** +19 **HP** 195 **Resistances** acid 10, poison 10 **Life Bloom** ([ <%TRAITS%33%%> conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33) <%END>, [ <%TRAITS%40%%> death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) <%END>, [ <%TRAITS%134%%> primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134) When Akuzhail dies <%END>, its body explodes in a riot of seething vegetation and raw life. All living creatures within a 60-foot radius are restored 12d6 Hit Points from this energy. A fully grown [bloom of Lamashtu](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2206) emerges from the carcass, but it can take no actions on this round. Next round, this bloom of Lamashtu enters combat at the same point in the turn during which Akuzhail perished on the previous round, but the bloom is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) 1. At the end of the bloom's turn, it attempts a DC 11 flat check) on a success it loses the slowed 1 condition at the start of its next turn.
Of Three Minds** **Trigger** Akuzhail fails or critically fails a Will save** **Effect** Akuzhail rerolls the Will save with a –4 penalty. If it fails this second save, it may make a third attempt but with a –8 penalty. It cannot make a third attempt if its second attempt was a critical failure.
**Three Headed** Any ability that would sever a chimera's head (such as a critical hit with a vorpal weapon) severs one head at random. Losing a head doesn't kill a chimera (as long as it has one head left), but it does prevent it from making Strikes with the lost head or using the head's Breath Weapon.
**Triple Opportunity** A chimera gains 2 extra reactions each round that it can use only to make Attacks of Opportunity. It must use a different head for each reaction, and it can't use more than one on the same triggering action. If it loses one of its heads, it also loses one of these extra reactions.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 40 feet, bloom stride **Melee** vulture beak +23, **Damage** 2d8+11 slashing plus1d8 bleed **Melee** snake fangs +23, **Damage** 2d12+11 piercing plus bloom venom **Melee** centipede mandibles +23, **Damage** 2d6+11 piercing plus 2d6 acid **Melee** claw +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d8+11 slashing **Bloom Stride** Akuzhail can move through the undergrowth in area C10 without issue and does not treat this cavern as difficult terrain. **Bloom Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Saving Throw** Fortitude DC 29; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d6 poison and enfeebled 1; **Stage 2** 2d6 poison and enfeebled 2; **Stage 3** 2d10 poison, enfeebled 2, and exposure to [Lamashtu's bloom](/Curses.aspx?ID=41). **Breath Weapon** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) Akuzhail breathes a 60-foot line of venom from its snake mouth that deals 11d6 poison damage (DC 29 basic Fortitude save). Akuzhail can't use Breath Weapon for 1d4 rounds. **Frightening Rattle** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) Akuzhail shakes the rattle at the end of its tail to distract and frighten its enemies. Any creature within 30 feet must succeed at a DC 29 Will save or become [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) 1 (frightened 2 on a critical failure). On a critical success, a creature is temporarily immune to Akuzhail's Frightening Rattle for 24 hours. **Three-Headed Strike** Akuzhail makes a Strike with its vulture beak, snake fangs, and centipede mandibles, each at a –2 penalty and targeting a different creature. These Strikes count as only one attack for its multiple attack penalty, and the penalty doesn't increase until it has made all three attacks.
name: Akuzhail

markdown:
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 33 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 [River Elasmosaurus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2305) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 275 **Perception** +21; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +22 **Str** +7 **Dex** +5 **Con** +6 **Int** -4 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +0 **Deep Breath** The elasmosaurus can hold its breath for 2 hours. --- **AC** 31 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +18 **Will** +21 **HP** 195 **Long Neck** An elasmosaurus's long neck allows it to interact with the surface while its body remains submerged underwater. While submerged no deeper than 15 feet underwater, an elasmosaurus can still stick its head up to breathe. An elasmosaurus gains [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459) against attacks made against creatures who are above the water's surface while it is underwater, even if its head is above the surface.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** Jaws only. --- **Speed** 5 feet, swim 35 feet **Melee** jaws +24 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d12+13 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** paddle +24, **Damage** 3d6+13 bludgeoning **Drag Below** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) The elasmosaurus attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against a [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) foe's Fortitude DC. If the elasmosaurus succeeds, the foe is forcibly moved 5 feet toward the elasmosaurus's body. If the elasmosaurus critically succeeds, the foe is moved 10 feet towards the elasmosaurus's body. **Thrashing Retreat** A swimming elasmosaurus thrashes the area around it as it attempts to flee. It makes two paddle Strikes, each of which must be against separate targets, and each of which takes the normal multiple attack penalty. It then [Swims](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) up to its swim Speed. This Swim does not trigger reactions based on movement.
name: River Elasmosaurus

**Drain Lintwerth** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11)) **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** Imeckus hasn't yet acted on his turn and Lintwerth is in an adjacent square; **Effect** Imeckus draws power from his bond with his familiar. During his turn, he gains the ability to cast one spell he prepared today and already cast, without spending a spell slot. He must still Cast the Spell and meet the spell's other requirements. 

**Steady Spellcasting**   If a reaction would disrupt Imeckus's spellcasting action, he attempts a DC 15 flat check; if he succeeds, his action isn't disrupted.

**Recall Lintwerth** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) Imeckus calls out Lintwerth's name and snaps his fingers, causing his dust mephit familiar (and up to 1 Bulk of objects carried by Lintwerth) to instantly transport to an adjacent square of Imeckus' choice, providing Lintwerth is within a mile.

**Quickened Casting** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Metamagic](/Traits.aspx?ID=107)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** If Imeckus's next action is to cast a cantrip or spell that is 4th level or lower, reduce the number of actions to cast it by 1 (minimum 1 action).

**Enhanced Familiar**   Imeckus Stroon's familiar [Lintwerth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2310) is a loyal companion and is a bit more powerful than the standard familiar in that he does not possess the minion trait; Imeckus can direct Lintwerth's actions verbally as a free action. If Lintwerth is slain, Imeckus becomes [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 for 24 hours.

</column>
name: Imeckus Stroon

**Release Jewel** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) With a command to [Jewel](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2313), Pavetta releases her to serve as a minion. She must concentrate to sustain this ability, during which she continues to shout commands to the little dog.

**Baroness's  Scream** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) Pavetta attempts a single Intimidation check to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) and applies the result to every opponent within 30 feet. Additionally, she automatically alerts any allies within 100 feet that are able to hear her to her need for assistance. The baroness can't Scream for 1 round.

**Stabbing  Fit** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Requirement** Pavetta is affected by Vengeful Rage. **Effect** Pavetta makes up to five melee Strikes with her dagger, each  against  a  different target. These attacks count toward her multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after she makes all of her attacks.

</column>
name: Pavetta Stroon-Drelev

All creatures other than Pavetta within 20 feet must attempt a DC 25 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and becomes temporarily immune to Distracting Yipping for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature takes a –2 status penalty to Perception checks for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature takes a –4 status penalty to Perception checks and is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but also, if the creature attempts any concentrate action during the round, they must succeed at a DC 11 flat check or the action is lost. **Return to Pavetta** If Pavetta doesn't sustain her Release Jewel action, Jewel can only take Stride actions to return to Pavetta's square. If she's in her square, she takes no actions.

**[Recall Knowledge - Ooze](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 35 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 [Fen Pudding](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2316) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 317 **Perception** +15; motion sense 60 feet, no vision **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +26, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +23 **Str** +7 **Dex** -5 **Con** +7 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -5 **Motion Sense** A black pudding can sense nearby motion through vibration and air movement. --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +11 **Will** +16 **HP** 300 **Immunities** acid, critical hits, mental, piercing, precision, slashing, unconscious, visual **Corrosive Mass** A creature that hits the pudding with a metal or wooden weapon must succeed at a DC 22 Reflex save or the weapon takes 2d6 acid damage (after dealing damage to the black pudding as normal). Thrown weapons and ammunition take this damage automatically with no save.
**Split** When the black pudding is hit by an attack that would deal slashing or piercing damage and has 10 or more HP, it splits into two identical puddings, each with half the original's HP. One pudding is in the same space as the original, and the other appears in an adjacent unoccupied space. If no adjacent space is unoccupied, it automatically pushes creatures and objects out of the way to fill a space (the GM decides if an object or creature is too big or sturdy to push).
**20 feet, climb 20 feet** suction --- **Speed** 20 feet, swim 20 feet **Melee** pseudopod +26 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+12 bludgeoning plus 2d8 acid, corrosive touch, and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 1d8+7 bludgeoning plus 1d6 acid, DC 26 **Corrosive Touch** When the pudding hits a creature with its pseudopod, any acid damage is dealt to the creature's armor or clothing as well as the creature. **Suction** The pudding can climb on ceilings and other inverted surfaces, though such surfaces are difficult terrain for it.

**Critical Failure** The creature is confused for as long as they remain within 30 feet of the Sister, or until they succeed at the DC 11 flat check to recover.
name: Sister Of The Bloodshot Eye

**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 29 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 [Skeletal Tiger Lord](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2320) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 327 **Perception** +18 **Languages** [Hallit](/Languages.aspx?ID=37) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +18 **Str** +6 **Dex** +4 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +0 **Items** [Breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10), [Composite Longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=74) (20 arrows), [Greatsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=24) --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +16 **Will** +16 **HP** 99 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15) 10) **Immunities** death effects, disease, mental, paralyzed, poison, unconscious **Resistances** cold 10, electricity 10, fire 10, piercing 10, slashing 10 **Rejuvenation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When a skeletal Tiger Lord is destroyed, its bones clatter to the ground in a tangle and its armor and weapons crumble to dust. It automatically reforms with its armor and weapons intact (regaining a full quiver of 20 arrows) 1 hour later at 1 Hit Point, allowing its fast healing to resume healing it thereafter. If the trap in area **D5** is disabled, the skeleton loses this ability; otherwise, the only way to permanently destroy a skeletal Tiger Lord is to reduce it to 0 Hit Points with [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) damage or [good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83) damage.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** greatsword +20 ([versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 1d12+10 slashing plus 2d6 negative **Ranged** composite longbow +18 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=201)), **Damage** 1d8+10 piercing

</row>

**Weaknesses**
silver 15

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +28 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 2d10+10 piercing plus curse of the wererat

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 2d8+10 slashing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_oathbow_ +30 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [range 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [volley 30 ft.](/Traits.aspx?ID=201)),
**Damage** 2d8+10 piercing

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** <actions string="Single Action" />  (concentrate, polymorph, primal, transmutation); human with fist +24 for 1d4+10 bludgeoning, or Small rat with Speed 30 feet, climb 10 feet.

**Curse of the Wererat**  ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) This curse affects only humanoids. Saving Throw DC 34 Fortitude save. On each full moon, the cursed creature must succeed at another Fortitude save or turn into a wererat until dawn. The creature is under the GM's control and goes on a rampage for half the night before falling unconscious until dawn.

**Deadly Aim** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirement** Gaetane hasn't attempted a Strike during his turn yet; **Effect** Gaetane makes a ranged Strike against his target at a –2 penalty. He gains a +6 circumstance bonus to damage on that Strike.

**Distracting Shot**   If Gaetane critically hits a creature with a ranged weapon, or hits a creature at least twice on the same turn with a ranged weapon, and if that creature is one he has used his oathbow to swear an oath to destroy, that creature is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the stat of Gaetane's next turn.

**Hunted Shot** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** Gaetane makes two ranged oathbow Strikes against a target. If both hit the same target, combine their damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses. He applies his multiple attack penalty to each Strike normally.

**Moon Frenzy**  ([Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) When a full moon appears in the night sky, the wererat must enter hybrid form, can't Change Shape thereafter, becomes one size larger, increases its reach by 5 feet, and increases the damage of its jaws by 2. When the moon sets or the sun rises, the wererat returns to humanoid form and is fatigued for 2d4 hours.

**Targeting Shot** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Requirement** Gaetane is currently suffering a multiple attack penalty; **Effect** Gaetane aims carefully, then makes an oathbow Strike; he ignores the target's concealed condition and all cover.

</column>
name: Gaetane

**[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Overgrown Viper Vine](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2325) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 360 **Perception** +24; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) (precise in area A7, otherwise imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +29, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +26 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +7 **Int** -4 **Wis** +5 **Cha** -3 --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +26 **Will** +24 **HP** 290 **Resistances** poison 15 **Cold Vulnerability** When exposed to a cold effect, the viper vine is overwhelmed by lethargy, becoming slowed 1 for 1d4 rounds. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** jaws +29 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d6+13 piercing plus 3d6 poison **Melee** vine +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+13 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Captivating Pollen** ([Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) The viper vine releases a 60-foot emanation of invisible pollen that stays in the air for 5 rounds unless dispersed by a moderate or stronger wind. Each creature that enters or starts its turn in the area must attempt a DC 33 Will save or be captivated. The viper vine can't use Captivating Pollen for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Captivating Pollen for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) 1.
**Failure** The creature is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14), and it must spend each of its actions to move closer to the viper vine as expediently as possible while avoiding obvious dangers. If a captivated creature is adjacent to the viper vine, it stays still and doesn't act. It ceases to be fascinated if it's no longer in the pollen aura at the end of its turn.
**[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 3d8+10 bludgeoning, DC 35. **Grasping Roots** **Trigger** a creature attempts a Move action in area A7; **Requirement** the overgrown viper vine is in area A7; **Effect** The viper vine manipulates its roots to cause foliage around the triggering creature to suddenly seem to spring to life. The triggering creature must attempt a DC 35 Reflex saving throw.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success**The target treats the first square of its movement as difficult terrain.
**Failure** The target becomes [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) by the foliage, (escape DC 35).
**Critical Failure** As failure but the target also falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).
name: Overgrown Viper Vine

**Site Bound** A typical ghost can stray only a short distance from where it was killed or the place it haunts. A typical limit is 120 feet. Some ghosts are instead bound to a room, building, item, or creature that was special to it rather than a location.

</column>

---

([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), rejuvenation)

death effects, disease, paralyzed, poison, precision, unconscious

**Resistances**
all damage 15 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103))

**Rejuvenation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) Releasing the nereid and allowing her to leave this place (or, alternatively, killing her) allows the Gardener to move on to the afterlife.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** fly 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
ghostly hand +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 4d8+12 negative

**Arcane Spontaneous Spells** DC 34, attack +28
- **Cantrips (8th)**
[Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Dancing Lights](/Spells.aspx?ID=58), [Electric Arc](/Spells.aspx?ID=97), [Ray of Frost](/Spells.aspx?ID=245), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334)
- **1st**
[Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244) (1 slot)
- **2nd**

- **3rd**

- **7th**
[Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=147) (1 slot)
- **8th**

**Corrupting Gaze** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The ghost stares at a creature it can see within 30 feet. The target takes 9d6 negative damage witth a DC 37 basic Will save. A creature that fails its save is also stupefied 1 for 1 minute.

**Draining Touch** <actions string="Two Actions" />  With a touch, the ghost attempts to drain a living creature's life force. It makes a ghostly hand Strike but deals no damage on a hit. Instead, the target becomes [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) 1 for 1 day, and the ghost regains HP equal to half its own level.

**Quickened Casting** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Metamagic](/Traits.aspx?ID=107)) **Frequency** once per day. If the Gardener's next action is to cast a 6th- or lower level spell, reduce the number of actions to cast it by 1 (minimum 1 action).

**Telekinetic Assault** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) The ghost cries out in pain and anguish as small objects and debris fly about in a 30-foot emanation. Creatures in this area take 9d6 bludgeoning , subject to a DC 37 basic Reflex save.

</column>

**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Ancient Wisp](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2327) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 364 **Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +23, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +20, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +21 **Str** -5 **Dex** +7 **Con** +0 **Int** +4 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +23 **Will** +20 **HP** 130 **Immunities** magic **Glow** ([aura](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=5), [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=171)) 30 feet. A will-o'-wisp is itself naturally invisible, but glows with a colored light, casting bright light in the aura and making it visible. An Ancient Wisp can also appear as a tiny pinpoint of light.
**Magic Immunity** A will-o'-wisp is immune to all spells except [_faerie fire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=107), [_glitterdust_](/Spells.aspx?ID=136), [_magic missile_](/Spells.aspx?ID=180), and [_maze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=187). --- **Speed** fly 50 feet **Melee** shock +23 ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d12+10 electricity **Feed on Fear** (concentrate) **Requirement** An enemy is under a fear effect or [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11) within 15 feet of the will-o'-wisp. **Effect** The will-o'wisp feeds on the creature's terror. It regains 2d8 Hit Points, and if it has Gone Dark, its glow reignites. A will-o'-wisp can take this action only once per round. **Go Dark** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) The will-o'-wisp extinguishes its glow, becoming invisible. It can end this effect with another use of this action. If it uses its shock attack while invisible, the arc of electricity lets any observer determine its location, making the will-o'-wisp only hidden to all observers until it moves.
name: Ancient Wisp
