**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 41 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 [Evindra](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2328) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 365 **Perception** +24; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Aquan](/Languages.aspx?ID=14), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +17, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +27, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +27, [First World Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +25, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +27 **Str** +0 **Dex** +8 **Con** +5 **Int** +4 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +8 --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +27 **Will** +24 **HP** 210 **Immunities** poison **Weaknesses** cold iron 15 **Watery Transparency** ([primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157), [water](/Domains.aspx?ID=35)) When underwater, the nereid's body is invisible. The nereid can dismiss or resume this transparency as an action that has the [concentrate](/Actions.aspx?ID=683) trait. --- **Speed** 25 feet, swim 50 feet **Melee** poisonous touch +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 8d6 poison **Primal Innate Spells** DC 33 - **5th** [Control Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=51) - **6th** [Elemental Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=98) (×3; water only), [Restoration](/Spells.aspx?ID=258) (×3), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) - **7th** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) (×3), [Summon Elemental](/Spells.aspx?ID=320) (water elementals only) **Drowning Touch** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) The nereid touches a creature and causes water from its own body to flow into the creature's lungs. If the creature cannot breathe water, it must attempt a DC 33 Fortitude save.
**Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Drowning Touch for 24 hours.
**Failure** The creature becomes sickened 3 as it chokes on the water.
**Critical Failure** The creature chokes on the water and runs out of air. It falls unconscious and starts drowning. If the creature is above water, it recovers from drowning as soon as it succeeds at a saving throw against suffocation. **Manifest Shawl** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The nereid divests themself of part of their connection to the First World and imbues this essence into a flowing shawl that enables them to function on land. The nereid can Dismiss this effect as long as they are touching the shawl. As long as the shawl exists, the nereid gains the amphibious trait. A nonnereid who carries the shawl also gains the amphibious trait. If a nereid's shawl is destroyed rather than Dismissed, the nereid can't Manifest a Shawl for 24 hours. Evindra does not currently posses her shawl.
name: Evindra

**Vigorous Shake** **Trigger** The troll king takes persistent acid or fire damage. **Effect** The troll king shakes itself to remove persistent acid or fire effects. **Failure** The creature is frightened 2.

The troll guard attempts two battleaxe Strikes, each of which must target a different foe within reach. These attacks count toward the troll's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after the troll makes both attacks.

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 [General Avinash Jurrg](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2330) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 385 **Perception** +26; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +24, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +26, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +28, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +28, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +28, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +24, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +24, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +26 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** +4 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 **Items** _+1 [glamered](/Equipment.aspx?ID=286) [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=147) [breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10)_, [_+1 composite longbow_](/Weapons.aspx?ID=74) (20 arrows), _[+2 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=380) [greatsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=24)_ --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +24 **Will** +24 +1 status on all saves **HP** 225 , regeneration 15 (deactivated by acid or fire) **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 30 feet **Melee** _greatsword_ +30 ([versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 1d12+12 slashing **Ranged** _composite longbow_ +28 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=191), [volley 30 ft.](/Traits.aspx?ID=201)), **Damage** 1d8+4 piercing **Primal Innate Spells** DC 36 - **2nd** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59) (at will), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (at will) - **4th** [Gaseous Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=129), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164), [Sleep](/Spells.aspx?ID=288) - **6th** [Chilling Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=36) - **7th** [Cone of Cold](/Spells.aspx?ID=47) **Battlefield Command** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** General Jurrg roars out a simple command during battle, using his supernatural power to force a foe within 30 feet to pursue a tactic in combat. The targeted creature must attempt a DC 34 Will save and is thereafter temporarily immune to new Battlefield Commands for 24 hours. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) 1 as their mind rails against the command. **Failure** The creature must spend 1 action at the start of their turn to perform a [basic action](/Rules.aspx?ID=439) commanded by the general, chosen from those listed on pages 470–471 of the Pathfinder Core Rulebook.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature must spend all 3 actions to perform basic actions commanded by the general. At the end of their turn, they must save against Battlefield Command again to determine how the effect continues into their next turn, but each time this save is attempted the DC is reduced by 2. **Change Shape** (concentrate, polymorph, primal, transmutation) The onidoshi takes on the appearance of an ogre. This doesn't change their Speed or Strike attack and damage.
name: General Avinash Jurrg

**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 44 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 42 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 [Villamor Koth](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2331) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 347 **Perception** +27 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Hallit](/Languages.aspx?ID=37) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +30, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +26, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +27, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +27, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +25 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +3 **Items** _[+2 resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=147) [hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62)_, _[+2 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=380) [grievous](/Equipment.aspx?ID=298) [greataxe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=21)_, [Lance](/Weapons.aspx?ID=28) --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +27 **Ref** +23 **Will** +27 **HP** 350 **Deny Advantage** Villamor Koth's foes struggle to pass his defenses. He isn't flat-footed to hidden, undetected, or flanking creatures of his level or lower, or to creatures of his level or lower using surprise attack. However, these creatures can still help their allies flank.
**Raging Resistance** While raging, Villamor Koth gains resistance 7 to physical weapon damage (but not physical damage from other sources, such as unarmed attacks).
[ <%UMR%3%%> Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3) <%END> --- **Speed** 35 feet **Melee** _greataxe_ +30 ([Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194)), **Damage** 2d12+13 slashing **Melee** lance +28 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [jousting d6](/Traits.aspx?ID=186), [Reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 1d8+12 piercing **Axe Specialization** Villamor Koth gains the critical specialization effects for all [axes](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1) he wields. **Furious Finish** ([Rage](/Traits.aspx?ID=135)) Desperate to finish the fight, Villamor Koth pours all his rage into one final blow. He makes a Strike. If it hits, he gains a circumstance bonus to damage equal to the number of rounds remaining in his Rage (maximum 10). After this Strike, his Rage immediately ends and he is fatigued until he rests for at least 10 minutes. **Giant's Lunge** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Rage](/Traits.aspx?ID=135)) Villamor Koth extends his body and prepares to attack foes outside his normal reach. Until his rage ends, all his melee weapons and unarmed attacks gain reach 10. This doesn't increase the reach of any weapon or unarmed attack that already has the reach trait, but it does combine with abilities that increase his reach due to increased size. **Knockback** ([Rage](/Traits.aspx?ID=135)) **Requirements** Villamor Koth's last action was a successful Strike. **Effect** Villamor Koth pushes his foe back 5 feet, with the effects of a successful [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38). He can follow the foe as normal for a successful shove. **Rage** Villamor begins raging. He gains 19 temporary Hit Points. This frenzy lasts for 1 minute, until there are no enemies he can perceive, or until he falls unconscious, whichever comes first. He can't voluntarily stop raging. While he is raging, he deals 12 additional damage with melee weapons and unarmed attacks. This additional damage is halved if his weapon or unarmed attack is agile. While raging, Villamor Koth's AC is 33.
name: Villamor Koth

**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 42 **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 42 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 [Alasen](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2332) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 389 **Perception** +26; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); tiger empathy **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +25, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +28, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +25, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +25, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +21, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +20 **Str** +8 **Dex** +6 **Con** +5 **Int** +0 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +5 **Tiger Empathy** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) Alasen can communicate with felines. **Items** _+1 [greater invisibility](/Equipment.aspx?ID=287) [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=147) [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4)_, [ring of fire resistance](/Equipment.aspx?ID=455), [ring of cold resistance](/Equipment.aspx?ID=455) --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +28 **Will** +26 **HP** 245 **Resistances** cold 5, fire 5 **Weaknesses** silver 10 **Defensive Rol** **Frequency** once per 10 minutes **Trigger** A physical attack would reduce Alasen to 0 Hit Points. **Effect** Alasen drops into a roll to disperse the force of the blow and takes half damage from the attack. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** jaws +28, **Damage** 3d6+16 piercing plus curse of the weretiger **Melee** claw +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 3d4+16 slashing **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) Medium human with fist +26 for 2d6+16 bludgeoning, or tiger with Speed 30 feet and [Wrestle](/Monsters.aspx?ID=69). **Curse of the Weretiger** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) This curse affects only [humanoids](/Traits.aspx?ID=91); **Saving Throw** DC 18 Fortitude. On each full moon, the cursed creature must succeed at another Fortitude save or turn into the same kind of werecreature until dawn. The creature is under the GM's control and goes on a rampage for half the night before falling unconscious until dawn. **Critical Debilitating Strike** **Trigger** Alasen's Strike hits a flat-footed creature and deals damage. **Effect** The target becomes enfeebled 1 or takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speeds, as chosen by Alasen. On a critical hit, the target must also attempt a DC 34 Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) 1 until the end of Alasen's next turn.
**Failure** The target is slowed 2 until the end of Alasen's next turn.
**Critical Failure** The target is [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) until the end of Alasen's next turn. **Mobility** When Alasen takes a Stride action to move half her Speed or less, that movement does not trigger reactions. **Moon Frenzy** ([Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) When a full moon appears in the night sky, the werecreature must enter hybrid form, can't Change Shape thereafter, becomes one size larger, increases their reach by 5 feet, and increases the damage of their jaws Strike by 2. When the moon sets or the sun rises, the werecreature returns to humanoid form and is [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) for 2d4 hours. **Pounce** The weretiger Strides and makes a Strike at the end of that movement. If the weretiger began this action hidden, they remain [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) until after this ability's Strike. **[Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=30)** claw **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** Alasen deals 2d6 extra precision damage to flat footed creatures.
name: Alasen

**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Kellid Graveknight](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2336) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 401 **Perception** +27; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)darkvision **Languages** [Hallit](/Languages.aspx?ID=37) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +29, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +32, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +30, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +27, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +27 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 **Items** _+1 [greater frost](/Equipment.aspx?ID=296) [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=380) dagger_, _[resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=147) splint mail_, _+2 [greater frost](/Equipment.aspx?ID=296) [warhammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=47)_ --- **AC** 37 **Fort** +24 **Ref** +25 **Will** +27 **HP** 255 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), rejuvenation) **Immunities** cold, death effects, disease, paralyzed, poison, precision, and unconscious **Sacrilegious Aura** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [aura](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=5), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64)) 30 feet. When a creature in the aura uses a [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) spell or ability, the graveknight automatically attempts to counteract it, with a +23 counteract modifier.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _warhammer_ +30 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193)), **Damage** 3d8+16 bludgeoning plus 1d6 cold **Melee** _dagger_ +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 3d4+14 piercing plus 1d6 cold **Ranged** _dagger_ +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 1d4+14 piercing plus 1d6 cold **Devastating Blast** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) The graveknight unleashes a 30-foot cone of energy. Creatures in the area take 8d12 cold damage (DC 34 basic Reflex save). The graveknight can use this ability once every 1d4 rounds. **Repulsing Blow** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Requirements** The graveknight's last action was a success with a Strike using a shove weapon. **Effect** The target struck by the shove weapon is knocked back 10 feet and must succeed at a DC 33 Will save or be unable to approach within 5 feet of the graveknight for 1 round. If the last action was a critical hit, the target automatically fails the Will save. **Graveknight's Curse** This curse affects anyone who wears a graveknight's armor for at least 1 hour. **Saving Throw** DC 34 Will save; **Onset** 1 hour; **Stage 1** [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9) 1 and cannot remove the armor (1 day); **Stage 2** doomed 2, hampered 10, and cannot remove the armor (1 day); **Stage 3** dies and transforms into the armor's graveknight. **Weapon Master** The graveknight has access to the critical specialization effects of any weapons it wields.
name: Kellid Graveknight

**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 45 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 43 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 [Korog](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2337) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 403 **Perception** +29; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Hallit](/Languages.aspx?ID=37) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +29, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +33, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +30, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +29, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +29 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 **Items** _[+2 resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=147) [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13)_, _+2 [greater shock](/Equipment.aspx?ID=303) [maul](/Weapons.aspx?ID=33)_ --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +30 **Ref** +27 **Will** +27 **HP** 295 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), rejuvenation) **Immunities** death effects, disease, electricity, paralyzed, poison, precision, and unconscious **Sacrilegious Aura** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [aura](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=5), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64)) 30 feet. When a creature in the aura uses a [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) spell or ability, the graveknight automatically attempts to counteract it, with a +26 counteract modifier.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _greater shock maul_ +30 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193)), **Damage** 3d12+17 bludgeoning plus 1d6 electricity **Designate Blood-Foe** Korog declares one creature he can see within 30 feet to be his blood- foe whom he swears to defeat. He can have up to three blood-foes designated at a time; any additional blood- foe removes another blood-foe of Korog's choice. Korog treats the results of Perception checks made against blood-foes as improved by one degree, and he has a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against his blood-foes' attacks. **Devastating Blast** The graveknight unleashes a 30-foot cone of energy. Creatures in the area take 9d12 electricity damage (DC 37 basic Reflex save). The graveknight can use this ability once every 1d4 rounds. **Graveknight's Curse** This curse affects anyone who wears a graveknight's armor for at least 1 hour. **Saving Throw** DC 37 Will save; **Onset** 1 hour; **Stage 1** [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9) 1 and cannot remove the armor (1 day); **Stage 2** doomed 2, hampered 10, and cannot remove the armor (1 day); **Stage 3** dies and transforms into the armor's graveknight. **Korog's Command** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** Korog commands his allies to take an action. All allies who hear or see him are [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) 1 for 1 round and can use this extra action to Step, Stride, or make a Strike against one of Korog's blood-foes. **Weapon Master** The graveknight has access to the critical specialization effects of any weapons it wields.

markdown:
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Thresholder Hermeticist](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2338) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 407 **Perception** +29; unseen sight **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Hallit](/Languages.aspx?ID=37) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +29, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +31, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +25, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +27, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +31, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +27, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +27, [Yog-Sothoth Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +31 **Str** +2 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** +5 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +1 **Infused Items** A Thresholder Hermeticist carries the following infused items, which last for 24 hours or until the next time the Thresholder Hermeticist makes their daily preparations: infused [greater acid flasks](/Equipment.aspx?ID=74) (14), infused [major eagle-eye elixirs](/Equipment.aspx?ID=90) (2), infused [major elixirs of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=91) (3), infused [greater mistform elixirs](/Equipment.aspx?ID=96) (2). **Items** [expanded alchemist's tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2), [major antidote](/Equipment.aspx?ID=80), _[+2 greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=380) [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3)_, [religious symbol of Yog-Sothoth](/Equipment.aspx?ID=41), _[+2 resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=147) [studded leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=5)_ --- **AC** 39 concealed **Fort** +28 **Ref** +30 **Will** +26 juggernaut **HP** 290 **Resistances** mental 15 **Juggernaut** When the Thresholder Hermeticist rolls a success on a Fortitude save, they get a critical success instead.
**Outside of Time** The Thresholder Hermeticist exists outside of time; they don't age, don't need food or drink to survive, and can't be [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) or [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) unless they choose to be. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _dagger_ +31 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 3d4+8 piercing plus cruel anatomist **Ranged** greater acid flask +31 ([range increment 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [splash 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=150)), **Damage** 1 acid damage plus 3d6 [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and 3 acid [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage **Bomber** When throwing an alchemical bomb with the [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) trait, the Thresholder Hermeticist can deal splash damage to only their primary target instead of the usual splash area. **Cruel Anatomist** The Thresholder Hermeticist deals an additional 2d8 bleed damage with melee Strikes. A creature who bleeds for 2 rounds from this becomes [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) 1 for as long as it continues bleeding. **Quick Bomber** The Thresholder Hermeticist Interacts to draw an alchemical bomb, then Strikes with it.
Unseen Sight The ritual Foras led his cultists on allowed them to turn aside time and halt aging, but it required more than the sacrifice of an individual's given name—it required the sacrifice of sight itself. The removal of the eyes symbolized a Thresholder's abandonment of all they had seen before, and with the ritual's completion they gain something greater than mere vision: they gain the Unseen Sight. This is a precise sense that mimics darkvision yet functions even if the eye sockets themselves are covered. More importantly, the unseen sight allows a Thresholder to see the truth of the world, granting them the ability to see things as they actually are as if they were under the effects of true seeing, provided they are located within a region under the influence of Yog- Sothoth. Currently in the Stolen Lands, this is limited to the island of Candlemere and the dungeons within it; beyond this area, the Thresholder cultists are blind. Of course, if their plan to draw Yog-Sothoth into the region succeeds, the region in which the unseen sight functions will increase dramatically...
name: Thresholder Hermeticist

**9th** [Delay Consequence](/Spells.aspx?ID=622), [Stasis](/Spells.aspx?ID=623) **Divine Font** [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) **Reminder of Doom** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The mystic reminds their allies of the punishments awaiting those who fail Yog- Sothoth. The mystic and their allies within 60 feet gain a +2 status bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls until the end of the mystic's next turn. **Steady Spellcasting** If a reaction would disrupt the Thresholder Mystic's spellcasting action, they attempt a DC 15 flat check; on a success, the action isn't disrupted.

More importantly, the unseen sight allows a Thresholder to see the truth of the world, granting them the ability to see things as they actually are as if they were under the effects of true seeing, provided they are located within a region under the influence of Yog- Sothoth. Currently in the Stolen Lands, this is limited to the island of Candlemere and the dungeons within it; beyond this area, the Thresholder cultists are blind. Of course, if their plan to draw Yog-Sothoth into the region succeeds, the region in which the unseen sight functions will increase dramatically...

**Guarded Movement** The disciple gains a +4 circumstance bonus to AC against reactions triggered by their movement.
Outside of Time** The Thresholder Hermeticist exists outside of time** they don't age, don't need food or drink to survive, and can't be [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) or [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) unless they choose to be. --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** fist +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d6+11 bludgeoning plus 3d6 mental **Melee** lurker claw +29 ([Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=180), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Parry](/Traits.aspx?ID=190)), **Damage** 2d12+11 slashing plus 3d6 mental **Ranged** shuriken +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d4+11 piercing plus 3d6 mental **Flurry of Blows** ([Flourish](/Traits.aspx?ID=73)) **Frequency** Once per turn; **Effect** The disciple makes two unarmed Strikes. If both hit the same creature, combine their damages for the purposes of resistances and weaknesses. The disciple's multiple attack penalty applies normally to these Strikes. **Lurker Stance** ([Stance](/Traits.aspx?ID=152)) The disciple takes the stance of the Lurker at the Threshold, full of menace and danger. The disciple can make lurker claw unarmed attacks. These deal 1d12 slashing damage; are in the brawling group; and have the parry, forceful, and unarmed traits. While in this stance, if the disciple succeeds or critically succeeds at a check to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) a target, the target must succeed at a DC 34 Will saving throw or be [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) 1 (stupefied 3 on a critical failure) for 1 minute. **Ominous Mien** **Requirement** The disciple is in Lurker Stance; **Effect** The disciple gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Intimidation checks and their [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) attempts don't take a penalty for not speaking the target's language and don't cause the target to become temporarily immune. These effects last until the end of the disciple's next turn. **Temporal Strikes** The disciple's Strikes force the creature hit to endure a deluge of unfamiliar memories from a combination of past lives and parallel existences, causing an additional 3d6 mental damage with a successful hit.
Unseen Sight The ritual Foras led his cultists on allowed them to turn aside time and halt aging, but it required more than the sacrifice of an individual's given name—it required the sacrifice of sight itself. The removal of the eyes symbolized a Thresholder's abandonment of all they had seen before, and with the ritual's completion they gain something greater than mere vision: they gain the Unseen Sight. This is a precise sense that mimics darkvision yet functions even if the eye sockets themselves are covered. More importantly, the unseen sight allows a Thresholder to see the truth of the world, granting them the ability to see things as they actually are as if they were under the effects of true seeing, provided they are located within a region under the influence of Yog- Sothoth. Currently in the Stolen Lands, this is limited to the island of Candlemere and the dungeons within it; beyond this area, the Thresholder cultists are blind. Of course, if their plan to draw Yog-Sothoth into the region succeeds, the region in which the unseen sight functions will increase dramatically...
name: Thresholder Disciple

**Planar Fast Healing** A denizen of Leng maintains a connection to Leng at all times, and when away from Leng, they have [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15) 5. They lose this ability on Leng or in areas where planar connections do not function. If killed, their body dissolves into nothingness in 1d4 rounds, leaving behind their equipment. A slain denizen reforms in Leng they can be permanently killed only when their planar fast healing doesn't function. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** kukri +34 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 3d6+13 slashing plus 3d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Melee** jaws +32 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 3d10+13 piercing plus debilitating bite **Occult Innate Spells** DC 40, attack +34 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246) - **2nd** [Mirror Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=197) - **3rd** [Hypnotic Pattern](/Spells.aspx?ID=157), [Levitate](/Spells.aspx?ID=170) - **4th** [Outcast's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=211) - **5th** [Locate](/Spells.aspx?ID=173), [Mind Probe](/Spells.aspx?ID=193) - **7th** [Warp Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=369) - **8th** [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315), [Uncontrollable Dance](/Spells.aspx?ID=347) - **9th** [Unfathomable Song](/Spells.aspx?ID=349), [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=220) - **Constant (9th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Leng Ruby** ([Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) Many denizens of Leng carry strange rubies mined from quarries in Leng. As long as a creature holds a Leng ruby that it willingly accepted as a gift or payment from a denizen of Leng, any denizen of Leng can target that creature with [_mind reading_](/Spells.aspx?ID=194), [_outcast's curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=211), [_phantom pain_](/Spells.aspx?ID=220), or [_warp mind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=369) at a range of 1 mile, and the bearer uses an outcome one degree of success worse than the result of its saving throw against outcast's curse. **Debilitating Bite** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) A creature that takes damage from a denizen's bite must succeed at a DC 40 Fortitude save or become [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) 1. Each time a target fails an additional save against this ability, the condition value increases by 1 (to a maximum of clumsy 4). This condition value decreases by 1 every 24 hours. **Play the Pipes** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** The Leng envoy is holding a musical instrument; **Effect** The envoy plays a melody on its instrument to cast [_uncontrollable dance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=347), [_unfathomable song_](/Spells.aspx?ID=349), or [_warp mind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=369) without expending the spell slot and using his music in place of providing the spell's component actions. The spell gains the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) trait and targets all creatures in a 60-foot emanation instead of its usual targets. A creature that succeeds at its Will save against any spell is then temporarily immune from all spells played from the envoy's pipes for 1 minute. Aberrations are immune to this music.
name: Leng Envoy

On a critical failure, it also becomes [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1d4 rounds.
**Ripping Gaze** ([aura](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=5), [evocation](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1671), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) 30 feet. The hound of Tindalos's eyes glow balefully, causing painful but bloodless wounds to rip open in the body of a creature that meets its awful gaze. When a creature ends its turn in the aura's emanation, it takes 8d6 slashing damage (DC 33 basic Fortitude save). A creature that critically succeeds at its save is temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Vulnerable to Curved Space** When a hound of Tindalos is not adjacent to a structural angle of 90º (or more acute), its resistance to physical damage is suppressed and it becomes [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) 1. It can't recover from this sickened condition, but the condition ends automatically once the hound is again adjacent to a suitable angle. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 30 feet **Melee** jaws +29, **Damage** 3d12+14 **Melee** claws +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 3d10+14 **Occult Innate Spells** DC 32, attack +29 - **6th** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (self only), [Locate](/Spells.aspx?ID=173), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=289) - **7th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will), [Dimensional Anchor](/Spells.aspx?ID=70), [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=147) - **8th** [Discern Location](/Spells.aspx?ID=75) **Angled Entry** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) The hound of Tindalos casts a 7th-level [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) spell, but it must transport itself into a space adjacent to an angle of 90º (or more acute) in the structure or environment around it. For example, it could teleport to a space adjacent to a wall (using the angle between the wall and floor) or a corner in a room, or adjacent to a sizable tree growing straight up out of the ground, but not to a flat plain or a room with only curved corners and edges.
Once per day, the hound can use this ability to plane shift to or from the Dimension of Time, with the same restrictions on what angles it can appear next to.
name: Mastiff Of Tindalos

**[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 43 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 41 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 [Mandragora Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2345) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 430 **Perception** +30; blood scent, [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +32, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +29, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +34 **Str** +5 **Dex** +8 **Con** +6 **Int** -1 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +0 **Blood Scent** A mandragora can smell creatures with blood as an imprecise sense at a range of 30 feet, and it can smell [demons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=28), [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69), and sorcerers with blood as a precise sense at a range of 30 feet. --- **AC** 42 **Fort** +30 **Ref** +34 **Will** +28 **HP** 230 ([fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15) 15) **Immunities** precision, swarm mind **Resistances** bludgeoning 15, electricity 15, piercing 15, slashing 7 **Weaknesses** area damage 15, fire 15, splash damage 15 **Vulnerability to Supernatural Darkness** Whenever a mandragora begins its turn in an area of magical darkness, it is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) 1 on that turn. --- **Speed** 30 feet, burrow 10 feet, climb 30 feet **Swarming Bites** Each enemy in the swarm's space takes 7d8 piercing damage (DC 40 basic Reflex save) and is exposed to mandragora swarm venom. A successful save negates the poison exposure. **Blood Drain** **Trigger** An enemy takes damage from the mandragora swarm's Swarming Bites action; **Effect** The mandragora swarm drains blood from the creature, dealing 9d6 damage. If the creature is a [demon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=28), [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69), or sorcerer, the mandragora gains temporary Hit Points equal to the damage dealt. A creature that has its blood drained by a mandragora is drained 2 until it receives healing of any kind or amount. **Mandragora Swarm Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** DC 40 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 4d6 poison damage and stupefied 1 (1 round); **Stage 2** 4d6 poison damage, confused, and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) 1 (1 round); **Stage 3** 6d6 poison damage, [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5), and stupefied 1 (1 round) **Piercing Shriek** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The mandragora emits an unsettling shriek. Each non-mandragora creature within 30 feet must attempt a DC 40 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34).
**Failure** The creature is sickened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is sickened 2 and [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35). As long as the creature remains sickened, this slowed condition value can't be reduced below 1.
name: Mandragora Swarm

**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 48 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 46 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 43 [The Horned Hunter](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2346) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 431 **Perception** +35; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +35, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +35, [First World Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +32, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +33, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +35, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +33 **Str** +8 **Dex** +6 **Con** +6 **Int** +4 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +8 **Fey Conduit** Before she sent him into the Material Plane, Nyrissa used the power of her home within the Fable (see area M7c on page 476) to forge a link with the Horned Hunter. During his time on the Material Plane, she can observe the world as if seeing through his eyes and listening through his ears. She cannot bolster the Horned Hunter via this link, and uses it primarily to spy on the PCs and observe their tactics in preparation for what she hopes never occurs—their invasion of Thousandbreaths. If the Horned Hunter claims Briar, the sword causes this link to Nyrissa to end immediately.
**Sylvan Wine** ([enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) A satyr's wineskin magically enchants any alcohol inside. With an Interact action, a living creature can imbibe the alcohol and gain a +3 item bonus to Will saves for the following hour. When the wineskin is removed from a satyr's person, the magic remains only until the wine spoils. The wineskin holds up to eight drafts of wine. **Items** _[+2 greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=380) [grievous](/Equipment.aspx?ID=298) [thundering](/Equipment.aspx?ID=306) [darkwood](/Equipment.aspx?ID=273) [composite shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=75)_ (20 arrows), hunting horn, wineskin --- **AC** 42 **Fort** +32 **Ref** +30 **Will** +30 juggernaut **HP** 350 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 15 **Juggernaut** When the Horned Hunter rolls a success on a ortitude save, he gets a critical success instead. --- **Speed** 35 feet **Melee** horns +34 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 3d12+16 bludgeoning plus [Improved Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=28) **Ranged** composite shortbow +35 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 3d6+16 piercing plus 1d6 sonic **Primal Innate Spells** DC 37 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Dancing Lights](/Spells.aspx?ID=58), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [Inspire Competence](/Spells.aspx?ID=385), [Inspire Courage](/Spells.aspx?ID=386), [Triple Time](/Spells.aspx?ID=392) - **4th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Sleep](/Spells.aspx?ID=288) - **8th** [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315), [Tree Stride](/Spells.aspx?ID=343) **Bloom Step** The Horned Hunter ignores the effects of non-magical difficult terrain and that caused by a bloom. **Bow Specialist** The Horned Hunter gains the critical specialization effect for any bow he wields. **Hunt Prey** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) The Horned Hunter designates a single creature he can see or hear as his prey. He gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks when he [Seeks](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) his prey and a +2 circumstance bonus to Survival when he [Tracks](/Actions.aspx?ID=66) his prey, and he ignores the penalty for making ranged attacks within his second range increment against his prey. He can have only one creature designated as prey at a time. If he uses Hunt Prey against a creature when he already has a creature designated, the prior creature loses the designation and the new prey gains the designation. Otherwise, a designation lasts until his next daily preparations. **Fleet Performer** **Trigger** The Horned Hunter Plays the Pipes; **Effect** The Horned Hunter Steps or Strides. **Masterful Hunter** The Horned Hunter's multiple attack penalty for attacks against his hunted prey is –3 (–2 with an agile weapon) on his second attack in a turn, and –6 (–4 with an agile weapon) on his third or subsequent attacks. The first time he hits his hunted prey in a round, he deals 2d8 additional precision damage. **Play the Pipes** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Requirements** The satyr is holding a musical instrument. **Effect** The satyr plays a melody on his instrument to cast [_charm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=34), fear, [_sleep_](/Spells.aspx?ID=288), or [_suggestion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) without expending the spell slot and using his music in place of providing the spell's component actions. The spell gains the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) trait and targets all creatures in a 60-foot emanation instead of its usual targets. A creature that succeeds at its Will save against any spell is then temporarily immune from spells played from that satyr's pipes for 1 minute. Satyrs are immune to this music.
name: The Horned Hunter

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 46 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 43 [The Misbegotten Troll](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2349) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 437 **Perception** +31; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +35, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +35 **Str** +9 **Dex** +4 **Con** +8 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +6 **Items** chain of lovelies, _[+2 greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=380) [glaive](/Weapons.aspx?ID=20)_, _[+2 greater resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=147) [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13)_ --- **AC** 40 **Fort** +36 **Ref** +27 **Will** +27 **HP** 430 , regeneration 45 (deactivated by acid or fire) **Weaknesses** fire 20 **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**Vigorous Shake** **Trigger** The troll king takes persistent acid or fire damage. **Effect** The troll king shakes itself to remove persistent acid or fire effects. The troll king immediately attempts a DC 15 flat check to end the persistent damage that triggered this reaction. If the troll king succeeds at this check, it can immediately use Primordial Roar as part of this reaction. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** _glaive_ +35 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=180), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+17 slashing plus 3d8 piercing **Melee** jaws +35 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d12+23 piercing **Melee** claw +35 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+23 slashing **Forced Regeneration** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Requirements** The troll king's regeneration is not currently deactivated. **Effect** The troll king regains 25 HP. **Jangle the Chain** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The Misbegotten Troll shakes his chain of caged sprites, causing the supernatural cages to amplify their despair. All creatures other than the Misbegotten Troll or his smilodon pets within 30 feet must attempt a DC 40 Will save. The Misbegotten Troll can't Jangle the Chain again for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Jangle the Chain for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) 1 by despair.
**Failure** The creature is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) 1 by sadness, and then sickened 1 by despair once the stun effect ends.
**Critical Failure** The creature is stunned 3 by sadness, and then sickened 2 by despair once the stun effect ends. **Primordial Roar** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The troll king unleashes a bestial roar. Each non-troll creature within 100 feet must attempt a DC 40 Will save. The creature is then temporarily immune for 10 minutes.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) 1.
**Failure** The creature is frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is frightened 3.
This ability doesn't affect his pet black smilodons. **[Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=30)** claw **Unstoppable Charge** The troll king Strides twice in a straight line. It can make up to two claw Strikes during this movement and one jaws Strike at the end of its movement. It can't Strike the same creature more than once. **Vicious Ranseur** When the Misbegotten Troll attacks with his glaive, it manifests dozens of hooks and spikes along its length and blade. He deals an additional 3d8 piercing damage to any creature he strikes but inflicts 1d8 piercing damage to himself with each glaive Strike.
name: The Misbegotten Troll

**[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2)): DC 47 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 45 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 42 [Lesser Jabberwock](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2350) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 440 **Perception** +36; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) 120 feet, [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Gnomish](/Languages.aspx?ID=5), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +37, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +41, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +38, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +34, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +37 **Str** +10 **Dex** +6 **Con** +7 **Int** +2 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +6 **Planar Acclimation** The jabberwock always treats the plane it is currently located on as its home plane. --- **AC** 47 **Fort** +38 **Ref** +33 **Will** +36 **HP** 430 , regeneration 20 (deactivated by vorpal weapons) **Immunities** paralyzed, sleep **Resistances** fire 20 **Weaknesses** vorpal weapons 20, vorpal fear **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 100 feet, DC 41
**Vorpal Fear** A jabberwock damaged by a vorpal weapon becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) 2 (or frightened 4 on a critical hit).
**Claws That Catch** **Trigger** A creature within the jabberwock's reach uses a [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) action or a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) action, leaves a square during a move action, makes a ranged attack, or uses a [concentrate](/Actions.aspx?ID=683) action **Effect** The jabberwock makes a claw Strike against the triggering creature. If the Strike hits, the jabberwock disrupts the triggering action. --- **Speed** 35 feet, fly 60 feet **Melee** jaws +38 ([deadly 2d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d10+18 piercing **Melee** claw +38 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d6+18 slashing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Melee** tail +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d8+18 bludgeoning plus [Improved Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=28) **Melee** wing +36 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d6+18 bludgeoning **Ranged** eyes of flame +38 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 8d6 fire plus 2d6 persistent fire **Primal Innate Spells** DC 44 - **Constant (10th)** [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Burble** The jabberwock creates a blast of strange noises and shouted nonsense in the various languages it knows (and invariably some languages it doesn't know), creating one of two effects. The jabberwock can't Burble again for 1d4 rounds.
  • **Confusion** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) 60 feet. Each creature in the emanation must succeed at a DC 44 Will save or become [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1d4 rounds.
  • **Sonic Beam** ([evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) The jabberwock focuses its Burbling into a 60.foot line of sonic energy that deals 22d6 sonic damage to creatures in the area (DC 44 basic Reflex save).
**Jaws that Bite** If the jabberwock makes a jaws attack and rolls a natural 19 on the d20 roll, the attack is a critical hit. This has no effect if the 19 would be a failure. **Whiffling** ([Aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206)) **Trigger** The jabberwock Flies or makes a wing Strike; **Effect** The jabberwock's wings whiffle, creating [severe winds](/Rules.aspx?ID=643) within a 30-foot emanation. These winds move outward from the jabberwock, and they persist until the start of the jabberwock's next turn. During this time, flight of any kind in the emanation requires a successful DC 41 [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) check to [Maneuver in Flight](/Actions.aspx?ID=30), and creatures flying toward the jabberwock are moving through greater difficult terrain. Creatures on the ground in the emanation must succeed at a DC 41 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to approach the jabberwock.
name: Lesser Jabberwock

**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 49 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 47 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 44 [Kargstaad](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2352) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 448 **Perception** +33; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23)Languages Aklo, Common, Jotun, Sylvan **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +39, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +33, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +37, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +33 **Str** +10 **Dex** +6 **Con** +8 **Int** +4 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +8 **Items** _[+2 greater resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=147) [hide armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=7)_, _[+2 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=380) [hatchets](/Weapons.aspx?ID=27)_ (4), [sack with 5 rocks](/Equipment.aspx?ID=45) --- **AC** 43 **Fort** +35 **Ref** +31 **Will** +33 juggernaut **HP** 360 ([fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15) 20) **Immunities** cold **Weaknesses** fire 20 **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**[Catch Rock](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=7)**
**Juggernaut** When Kargstaad rolls a success on a Fortitude save, he gets a critical success instead. --- **Speed** 30 feet; ice stride **Melee** _hatchet_ +37 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194)), **Damage** 3d6+18 slashing plus 2d6 cold **Ranged** rock +35 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d10+18 bludgeoning **Chill Breath** ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The frost giant breathes out a 15-foot cone of freezing moisture that quickly condenses into ice, dealing 9d6 cold damage. Each creature in the cone must attempt a DC 31 basic Reflex save. A creature that fails its save is also [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) and takes 2d6 cold damage at the end of each of its turns until it gets free (Escape DC 31). The giant can't use Chill Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Hatchet Onslaught** **Requirement** Each of Kargstaad's four hands wields a hatchet. **Effect** Kargstaad Strides up to half his speed, then makes four hatchet Strikes, applying his multiple attack penalty as normal. **Ice Stride** A frost giant isn't impeded by difficult terrain caused by snow or ice, nor does it need to attempt Acrobatics checks to keep from falling on slippery ice **Rage** **Requirements** Kargstaad isn't fatigued or raging. **Effect** Kargstaad gains 25 temporary hit points, deals an extra 12 points of damage with thrown rocks, and deals an extra 6 points of damage with hatchets. He takes a –1 penalty to AC and can't use actions with the concentrate trait (except Seek) unless they also have the rage trait. **[Throw Rock](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=38)** **Wide Swing** Kargstaad makes a single hatchet Strike and compares the attack roll result to the ACs of up to two foes within its reach. This counts as two attacks for Kargstaad's multiple attack penalty.
name: Kargstaad

Each Strike must target a different creature. Each attack takes the normal multiple attack penalty. **Snack** A roc gains a +2 circumstance bonus to hit with its beak Strike if the target is grabbed or restrained in its talon. **Snatch** A roc can Fly at half Speed while it has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) in either or both of its talons, carrying that creature along with it.

**[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 50 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 48 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 45 [Immense Mandragora](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2354) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 450 **Perception** +33; blood scent, [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23)Languages Abyssal, Aklo, Common, Sylvan **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +35, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +35, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +38 **Str** +10 **Dex** +5 **Con** +6 **Int** +0 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +6 **Blood Scent** A mandragora can smell creatures with blood as an imprecise sense at a range of 30 feet, and it can smell [demons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=28), [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69), and sorcerers with blood as a precise sense at a range of 30 feet.
 --- **AC** 43 **Fort** +34 **Ref** +31 **Will** +35 **HP** 400 **Resistances** bludgeoning 20, electricity 20 **Weaknesses** fire 20 **Vulnerable to Supernatural Darkness** Whenever a mandragora begins its turn in an area of magical darkness, it is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) 1 on that turn. --- **Speed** 40 feet, burrow 10 feet, climb 40 feet **Melee** jaws +38, **Damage** 4d12+18 piercing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Melee** thorny vine +38 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d8+18 slashing plus immense mandragora venom **Blood Drain** **Requirements** The immense mandragora has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20); **Effect The** immense mandragora drains blood from the creature it has grabbed, dealing 12d6 damage. If the creature is a [demon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=28), [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69), or sorcerer, the mandragora gains temporary Hit Points equal to the damage dealt. A creature that has its blood drained by a mandragora is [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) 2 until it receives healing of any kind or amount. **Immense Mandragora Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** DC 42 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 4d6 damage and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) 1 (1 round); **Stage 2** 4d6 poison damage, [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5), and stupefied 1 (1 round); **Stage 3** 6d6 poison damage, confused, and stupefied 1 (1 round) **Apocalyptic Shriek** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The immense mandragora emits a devastating shriek. Each non-mandragora creature within 30 feet must attempt a DC 42 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) 1 and takes 8d8 mental damage.
**Failure** The creature is sickened 2 and takes 16d8 mental damage.
**Critical Failure** The creature is sickened 2, slowed 1, and takes 150 mental damage. As long as the creature remains sickened, this slowed condition value can't be reduced below 1.
name: Immense Mandragora

If the Strike hits, the target must attempt a DC 40 Will save.

**Failure** The target is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) and [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) as long as it remains [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34). **Screeching Advance** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The owlbear makes a Bloodcurdling Screech and Strides twice. All creatures within 80 feet of the owlbear at any point during this movement are subjected to the effects of Bloodcurdling Screech.
name: Winged Owlbear

**Bonds of Iron** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15), [Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) Once per day, an annis hag can cause a cage built of cold iron fingernails to spring out of nothingness at a range of up to 30 feet, attempting an Athletics check to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) against the target's Fortitude DC; if the target has a weakness to cold iron, the annis hag gains a +2 circumstance bonus to this check. Unlike a normal Grapple, the annis hag doesn't need to be within reach and can move as she pleases, and a successful attempt lasts until the creature escapes (DC 42), causing the cage to crumble into rust. Any creature can attempt to destroy the cage by attacking it. It has an AC of 40, Hardness 20, and 80 Hit Points.

**Change Shape** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The hag can take on the appearance of any Medium female humanoid. This doesn't change her Speed or her attack and damage bonuses with her Strikes but might change the damage type her Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning).

**[Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=30)** <actions string="Single Action" />  claw

**Word of Recall**   The Knurly Witch has used word of recall to bind herself to the House at the Edge of Time (area K16).

markdown:
**[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 39 **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 39 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 [Ghostly Guard](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2358) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 461 **Perception** +29; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +29, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +29, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +27, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +31 **Str** -5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +0 **Int** +4 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +6 **Shame** A ghostly guard suffers from shame (see below) when it attacks a target that appears to be an elf or a worshipper of Calistria. Forgive Foe can be attempted against a ghostly guard with a DC 39 Diplomacy check or Society check. If a ghostly guard is affected by a critical success from Forgive Foe, it sighs in relief as it fades away, its soul released into the afterlife.
**Site Bound** A typical ghost can stray only a short distance from where it was killed or the place it haunts. A typical limit is 120 feet. Some ghosts are instead bound to a room, building, item, or creature that was special to it rather than a location. --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +27 **Will** +29 **HP** 210 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), rejuvenation) **Immunities** death effects, disease, paralyzed, poison, precision, unconscious **Resistances** all damage 15 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) **Rejuvenation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) Slaying or forgiving Nyrissa allows the ghostly guards to move on to their long-delayed afterlives, as does achieving a critical success with Forgive Foe. --- **Speed** fly 30 feet **Melee** ghostly rapier +27 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 3d6+12 negative plus 2d6 bleed **Ectoplasmic Maneuver** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) The ghostly guard lashes out with a whipping strand of solid ectoplasm against a target within 15 feet, in an attempt to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41), [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38), or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) the foe. It uses its ghostly rapier Strike rather than an Athletics check to determine the results of this attempt. **Frightful Moan** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The ghost laments its fate, forcing each living creature within 30 feet to attempt a Will save. On a failure, a creature becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) 2 (or frightened 3 on a critical failure). On a success, a creature is temporarily immune to this ghost's frightful moan for 1 minute. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** A ghostly guard deals 3d6 extra precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.
The Power of Forgiveness Some of the creatures in the House at the Edge of Time—the ghostly guards and defaced naiad queens, but most notably Nyrissa herself—still harbor vestiges of goodness in their minds and souls that can be appealed to by PCs who seek to forgive them for their actions. A PC can learn about the Forgive Foe action accidentally if they seek to appeal to a creature in the House at the Edge of Time in this way on their own, or they can learn about it by closely observing how certain creatures act when they attack certain foes.
These foes have a Shame entry in their stat block. When such a foe attacks specific targets delineated in their Shame entry, they suffer a –2 status penalty on attack rolls and to all save DCs to resist their spells or attacks. Whenever such a creature takes an action that triggers this penalty, all creatures within 30 feet can attempt a Perception check against the acting creature's Deception DC. On a success, the creature attempting the check realizes the creature taking the action is suffering from guilt and shame at their own action; this reveals that a Forgive Foe action might help the acting creature recover from its plight.
name: Ghostly Guard

**Slip Between** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) The stalker teleports into an occupied space it can see within 50 feet and makes a melee Strike.

</column>

name: Afziaka Stalker

markdown:
An umbraex appears at first glance to be a massive, majestic bird, much like a [phoenix](/Monsters.aspx?ID=330). Instead of compassion and warmth, however, it seethes with cold and hate. The umbraex is among the largest of the nihilistic undead called darvakkas. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 47 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 45 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 42 [Umbraex](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2361) **Source** [Pathfinder #185: A Taste of Ashes](/Sources.aspx?ID=188) pg. 83 **Perception** +36; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21) 100 feet **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20); can't speak any language, [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +36, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +38, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +40, [Negative Energy Plane Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +37, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +36, [Shadow Plane Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +39 **Str** +9 **Dex** +7 **Con** +10 **Int** +8 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +8 --- **AC** 45 **Fort** +38 **Ref** +32 **Will** +35 **HP** 450 (ashen rise, [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), unconscious **Weaknesses** fire 15, good 15, [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379) 15 **Ashen Rise** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When an umbraex is destroyed, its body discorporates into fine ash before being restored, fully healed, 1d4 rounds later. An umbraex can't use this ability as long as its remains are within an area consecrated to a good deity or an area of [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407). The umbraex can't use Ashen Rise again for 1 day.
**Entropy's Shadow** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118)) 40 feet. Umbraex leak entropy and corruption from their very being. A living creature entering or starting its turn in the aura takes 8d6 negative damage with a DC 42 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. If it fails, it's also [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) for 1 minute and pulled 10 feet toward the umbraex.
**Sunlight Powerlessness** An umbraex caught in sunlight is [stunned 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) and [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3). --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 70 feet **Melee** beak +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d10+12 piercing plus 2d10 cold and 2d10 [persistent negative](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Melee** talon +38 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+10 piercing plus 2d10 cold and 2d10 [persistent negative](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Ranged** icy blast +37 ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 8d6 cold plus 2d10 [persistent negative](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 44 - **Cantrips (10th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66) - **4th** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59) (at will) - **7th** [Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) (to [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1); [Negative Energy Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=2); or [Shadow Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=11) only), [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) - **9th** [Eclipse Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=96), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) (×3) **Life-Siphoning Screech** ([Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The umbraex [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=94), shrieking as it passes its enemies. Creatures within 20 feet of the umbraex's flight path take 22d6 mental damage depending on their DC 45 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and is [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10).
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is drained 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and is drained 2 and [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9).
The Bound One The secret of Yled's necromantic defenses is a group of darvakkas. The most powerful of these is a captive umbraex darvakka known only as “the Bound One.” Fortunately for life across the world, the Bound One is the only known umbraex on Golarion.
name: Umbraex

markdown:
Designed to serve as devastatingly powerful foot soldiers against enemies with plentiful magic, facetbound nullifiers shear through magical effects as easily as they slice through flesh and bone. The _[pale lavender ellipsoid aeon stones](/Equipment.aspx?ID=407)_ they bear are sunk deep into their heads and surrounded by an ugly bruise. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 39 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 [Facetbound Nullifier](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2362) **Source** [Pathfinder #185: A Taste of Ashes](/Sources.aspx?ID=188) pg. 84 **Perception** +25; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +27, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +31, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +27 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** +4 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +4 **Implanted Stone** A nullifier's aeon stone can't be [Disarmed](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) or removed until the nullifier is destroyed. **Items** _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [halberd](/Weapons.aspx?ID=26)_, [_pale lavender ellipsoid aeon stone_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=407), _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13)_ --- **AC** 38 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +22 **Will** +24 +2 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) **HP** 305 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) Always Ready The nullifier gains an additional reaction each round that can be used only for Defensive Dispel.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** The nullifier can also make attacks of opportunity against concentrate actions to Cast a Spell, and it disrupts that action on any hit, not just a critical hit.
**Defensive Dispel** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11)) **Trigger** The nullifier is the target of a spell; **Effect** The nullifier attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the triggering spell, with a counteract modifier of +27. This can be used only on spells that target the nullifier, not area spells that don't have targets. If it succeeds, it counteracts the spell for all targets, if other creatures were targeted in addition to the nullifier. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 25 feet **Melee** _halberd_ +30 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d10+16 piercing plus 2d10 force **Melee** fist +30 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d4+16 bludgeoning plus 2d10 force **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 34 - **Cantrips (8th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246) - **8th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) (×3) **Field of Force** A nullifier's melee Strikes deal an additional 2d10 force damage (already included in the Strikes above). **Sever Spell** ([Abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The nullifier makes a melee Strike against a target. If the attack hits, the nullifier attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) a spell or magical effect on the target (of the nullifier's choice), with a counteract modifier of +27. The target is temporarily immune to Sever Spell for 24 hours.
name: Facetbound Nullifier

<title level="1" pfs="">[Facetbound Cascader](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2363)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Facetbound cascaders must have been arcane spellcasters in life; in undeath, they draw upon the power of embedded [_orange prism aeon stone_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=407) to defend themselves from magical damage and redirect that energy toward their opponents. Despite their incredible intelligence, facetbound cascaders have few memories of their former lives and typically serve the whims of their creator.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 40

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Facetbound_Cascader.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 16">[Facetbound Cascader](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2363)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="NE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=160" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #185: A Taste of Ashes](/Sources.aspx?ID=188) pg. 85

**Perception** +28; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +28, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +31, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +27, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +29, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +28, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +28

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +6

**Con** +5

**Int** +9

**Wis** +6

**Cha** +5
</row>

**Implanted Stone** A cascader's aeon stone can't be [Disarmed](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) or removed until the cascader is destroyed.

**Items**
[_orange prism aeon stone_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=407), [Staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=12)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 38 

**Fort** +25 

**Ref** +26 

**Will** +28 

+2 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 280
([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42))

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Resistances**
[cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 15 (see Energy Ward)

**Energy Ward** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** The facetbound cascader would take acid, cold, electricity, fire, or poison damage; **Effect** The cascader gains resistance 15 to the triggering damage, replacing any other resistance gained from this ability. Most facetbound cascaders begin combat with resistance 15 to cold.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
staff +30 ([two-hand d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=198)),
**Damage** 1d4+9 bludgeoning plus energy charge

**Arcane Spontaneous Spells** DC 38, attack +30
- **Cantrips (8th)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Electric Arc](/Spells.aspx?ID=97), [Produce Flame](/Spells.aspx?ID=236), [Ray of Frost](/Spells.aspx?ID=245), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280)
- **1st**
[Color Spray](/Spells.aspx?ID=44), [Gust of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=143), [True Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=345) (3 slots)
- **2nd**
[Blur](/Spells.aspx?ID=28), [Continual Flame](/Spells.aspx?ID=50), [See Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=271) (3 slots)
- **3rd**
[Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=94), [Stinking Cloud](/Spells.aspx?ID=309), [Wall of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=367) (3 slots)
- **4th**
[Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=119), [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=125), [Resilient Sphere](/Spells.aspx?ID=255) (3 slots)
- **5th**
[Cloudkill](/Spells.aspx?ID=42), [Cone of Cold](/Spells.aspx?ID=47), [Magic Missile](/Spells.aspx?ID=180) (3 slots)
- **6th**
[Acid Arrow](/Spells.aspx?ID=2), [Chain Lightning](/Spells.aspx?ID=33), [Spellwrack](/Spells.aspx?ID=298) (3 slots)
- **7th**
[Disintegrate](/Spells.aspx?ID=76), [Power Word Blind](/Spells.aspx?ID=226), [Prismatic Spray](/Spells.aspx?ID=233) (3 slots)
- **8th**
[Horrid Wilting](/Spells.aspx?ID=152), [Polar Ray](/Spells.aspx?ID=224), [Power Word Stun](/Spells.aspx?ID=228) (3 slots)

**Energy Charge**  ([Abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11)) The cascader's melee Strikes deal an additional 4d10 damage of the type resisted by its Energy Ward.

**Energy Shift** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Metamagic](/Traits.aspx?ID=107)) The cascader can alter its spell damage. If the next action it uses is to Cast a Spell that deals acid, cold, electricity, fire, or poison damage, the cascader can change the damage type to match its Energy Ward instead of the spell's normal damage type (replacing the spell's corresponding trait).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Aeon Synergies</title>

The ritual used to create facetbound cascaders imbues them with a close connection to destructive magic; elemental spells are often the most destructive magic at hand.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-327" />
name: Facetbound Cascader
