<title level="1" pfs="">[Necrohulk Flailer](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2364)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Additional limbs, failing tentacles, and rubbery bodies are common among Mana Wastes mutants, and aspiring necrohulk creators like to combine flesh from more than one of these kinds of mutants to create gangly but powerful warriors.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 33

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Necrohulk_Flailer.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 13">[Necrohulk Flailer](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2364)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="NE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Mutant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=229" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=160" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #185: A Taste of Ashes](/Sources.aspx?ID=188) pg. 86

**Perception** +23; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +27, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +26

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +7

**Dex** +8

**Con** +5

**Int** -4

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +0
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 32 

**Fort** +23 

**Ref** +26 

**Will** +20 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 295
([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42))
, regeneration 15 (deactivated by [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3))

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Weaknesses**
slashing 15

**Alchemical Cartridge** ([alchemical](/Traits.aspx?ID=6)) Critical hits rupture the necrohulk's alchemical cartridge. The first time the necrohulk takes a critical hit, it loses its regeneration. The second time it takes a critical hit, it takes 15 [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) that it can't heal unless the cartridge is stitched back into place.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, climb 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tentacle +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d8+13 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +26 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d12+13 bludgeoning

**Alchemical Amplification** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([Alchemical](/Traits.aspx?ID=6)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** The necrohulk draws upon alchemical stores to push its body into overdrive. It becomes [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) for 1d4 rounds. It can use this extra action to Step, Stride, or Strike.

**[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)**   6d6 bludgeoning, DC 32

**Tentacle Storm** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The necrohulk flailer makes up to four tentacle Strikes, each against a different target. These attacks count toward its multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after the necrohulk makes all of these attacks.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Eerie Infiltrators</title>

Necrohulk flailers can fit into shockingly small spaces and are often commanded to wriggle up storm drains or through narrow crevices to attack foes. As they don't possess much independent initiative, however, necrohulk flailers can get confused or stuck, posing a danger to anyone who happens to come across them, even months or years later.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-328" />
name: Necrohulk Flailer

Mutants with incredible strength make the most powerful necrohulks. These necrohulk smashers are so swollen with biological and alchemical muscle enhancements that they can simply pound their way through enemies. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Necrohulk Smasher](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2365) **Source** [Pathfinder #185: A Taste of Ashes](/Sources.aspx?ID=188) pg. 87 **Perception** +25; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +32, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +27 **Str** +9 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** -4 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +29 **Ref** +23 **Will** +26 **HP** 345 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) , regeneration 15 (deactivated by [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Weaknesses** slashing 15 **Alchemical Cartridge** ([alchemical](/Traits.aspx?ID=6)) Critical hits rupture the necrohulk's alchemical cartridge. The first time the necrohulk takes a critical hit, it loses its regeneration. The second time it takes a critical hit, it takes 15 [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) that it can't heal unless the cartridge is stitched back into place.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** fist +30 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d10+15 bludgeoning and [Improved Knockback](/Feats.aspx?ID=470) **Ranged** rock +25 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d10+15 bludgeoning **Alchemical Amplification** ([Alchemical](/Traits.aspx?ID=6)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** The necrohulk draws upon alchemical stores to push its body into overdrive. It becomes [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) for 1d4 rounds. It can use this extra action to Step, Stride, or Strike. **[Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=30)** fist **Smashing Blow** The necrohulk smasher makes a fist Strike. If it hits, the necrohulk smashes the target into the floor or nearby wall. The target takes an additional 6d10 bludgeoning damage, depending on its DC 33 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The target takes half of the additional damage.
**Success** The target takes half the additional damage and is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36).
**Failure** The target takes full additional damage, is stunned 3, and knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).
**Critical Failure** The target takes double the additional damage, is stunned 3, and knocked prone. **[Throw Rock](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=38)**
Undead Fleshwarps Muscular [fleshwarps](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=31) are the most common ancestry used to make necrohulk smashers. These people have often already experienced lives of rejection or exclusion before the final indignity of unwanted reanimation.
name: Necrohulk Smasher

Necromancers reaching for the greatest heights of experimentation know that creating a new terror requires exacting, careful work at every stage of the process. In the case of the tenebric giant, this process begins with a [tomb giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1168) who dies after consuming a dose of several poisons combined with the [_shadow ash_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1723) reagent. This creates the right circumstances for a necromancer to raise them as a tenebric giant. They're particularly obedient to the will of the necromancer who created them and are charged full of energy from the [Shadow Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=11), which grants them new powers and abilities. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 41 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 [Tenebric Giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2366) **Source** [Pathfinder #185: A Taste of Ashes](/Sources.aspx?ID=188) pg. 88 **Perception** +31; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21) 80 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) (with its creator only) 100 feet **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +33, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +30, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +30, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +29 **Str** +9 **Dex** +4 **Con** +9 **Int** +4 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +5 **Items** _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [scythe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=39)_ --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +32 **Ref** +27 **Will** +30 **HP** 360 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Weaknesses** [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) 15 **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**[Catch Rock](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=7)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _scythe_ +34 ([deadly 2d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 3d10+17 slashing plus cloying shadows **Melee** claw +32 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d6+17 slashing plus cloying shadows **Ranged** rock +32 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d6+17 bludgeoning plus cloying shadows **Cloying Shadows** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=143)) The tenebric giant's attacks leave behind a residue of energy from the [Shadow Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=11). A creature hit by the tenebric giant's melee Strike takes an additional 1d8 [persistent negative damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and must attempt a DC 36 Reflex save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) until its next turn begins.
**Failure** The creature is blinded for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** The creature is permanently blinded. **Tenebric Stride** ([Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=143)) The tenebric giant can move quickly in shadowy conditions. The tenebric giant Strides twice and makes a scythe Strike at any point during this movement against a target that is in [darkness](/Rules.aspx?ID=409) or [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408). This movement doesn't provoke reactions. **[Throw Rock](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=38)**
Shadowy Cousins [Shadow giants](/Monsters.aspx?ID=681) look upon tenebric giants with disdain and fear. They view these undead creatures not as giants but as a corruption of the true nature of the Shadow Plane, closer to [darvakkas](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=305) than kin. They tend to give tenebric giants a wide berth, unwilling to confront the powerful creatures.
name: Tenebric Giant

Theater phantasms are amalgamations of the spiritual and emotional energy given up by entertainers and audiences of powerful performances. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Theater Phantasm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2367) **Source** [Pathfinder #185: A Taste of Ashes](/Sources.aspx?ID=188) pg. 89 **Perception** +32; [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=1), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21) 100 feet, [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) 100 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20); up to four others **Skills** [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +30, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +35, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +30, [Theater Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +36 **Str** -5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** +2 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +9 **Bonded Theater** A theater phantasm can't travel more than 50 feet from the theater (or similar location) where it was created. --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +25 **Will** +30 **HP** 260 (emotional consumption, dramatic return, [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** all 10 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive^](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) **Catharsis** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) When a theater phantasm is destroyed, it emits waves of emotional energy. All creatures within 50 feet take 10d6 mental damage (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save) as they experience a crushing barrage of emotions.
**Dramatic Return** ([necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) A destroyed theater phantasm slowly reforms to full strength over the course of 1 week. The only way to permanently destroy the phantasm is to reduce it to 0 Hit Points during a performance occurring in its bonded theater.
**Emotional Consumption** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) If a theater phantasm is within 100 feet of an audience of 20 or more intelligent beings witnessing a performance in its bonded theater, it gains [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15) 20. --- **Speed** fly 40 feet **Melee** spectral hand +30 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d10+15 negative **Ranged** impromptu props +28 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d12+18 bludgeoning **Spotlight** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The theater phantasm draws attention to a creature it admires. The phantasm designates a target other than itself within 50 feet with a subtle spotlight, giving the target a +2 status bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks and resistance 10 to mental damage for 1 minute. If it uses this ability again on a new target, the former creature loses its spotlight.
Competing Phantasms On very rare occasions, more than one theater phantasm takes up residence in the same theater, each trying to outdo the other.
name: Theater Phantasm
resistance_markdown: all 10 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive^](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103))

**Shadow Doubles** <actions string="Single Action to Three Actions" /> ([Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=143)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** For each action spent to use this ability, the ankou creates one shadowy duplicate of themself anywhere within 60 feet of themself. Shadow doubles have the same statistics as an ankou, but they have the [summoned](/Traits.aspx?ID=154) trait, have 110 Hit Points, can't use Shadow Doubles or innate spells, and have an attack bonus of +27 for their Strikes. A shadow double that attempts a saving throw against a light effect can't get a result better than failure. Each double remains for 1 round, until it's reduced to 0 Hit Points, or until it moves further than 120 feet from the ankou, whichever comes first. Each round thereafter, the ankou can spend a single action that has the [concentrate](/Actions.aspx?ID=683) trait to extend the duration of surviving duplicates by 1 round, to a maximum duration of 1 minute. The ankou can see through the eyes of all of the shadow doubles at once. A character who Seeks can identify an ankou as real or a shadow double with a successful DC 43 Perception check.

**[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)**   An ankou assassin's Strikes deal an additional 3d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.

[Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=125), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315)
- **5th**
[Black Tentacles](/Spells.aspx?ID=23), [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Mind Probe](/Spells.aspx?ID=193)
- **6th**
[Baleful Polymorph](/Spells.aspx?ID=17), [Feeblemind](/Spells.aspx?ID=112), [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344)
- **7th**
[Reverse Gravity](/Spells.aspx?ID=261), [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=119), [Veil](/Spells.aspx?ID=355)
- **8th**
[Contingency](/Spells.aspx?ID=49) (already cast), [Illusory Scene](/Spells.aspx?ID=161) (already cast), [Mind Blank](/Spells.aspx?ID=192) (already cast)
- **9th**
[Disjunction](/Spells.aspx?ID=77), [Magic Missile](/Spells.aspx?ID=180), [Project Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=237)
- **10th**
[Time Stop](/Spells.aspx?ID=339)

**Contingency** <actions string="Reaction" />  **Trigger** The Wriggling Man is reduced to 50 or fewer HP; **Effect** [_Dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), heightened to 5th level, teleports the Wriggling Man to area M6.

**Effortless Concentration** <actions string="Free Action" />  **Trigger** The wriggling man's turn begins. **Effect** The Wriggling Man immediately gains the effects of the Sustain a Spell action, allowing him to extend the duration of an active spell.

**Quickened Casting** <actions string="Free Action" />  **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** When the Wriggling Man casts a spell of 8th level or lower, reduce the number of actions to cast it by 1 (minimum 1 action).

**Squirming Embrace** <actions string="Single Action" />  The worm that walks Strides, ending its movement sharing a space with a creature, and deals 8d8 piercing damage to the creature. The creature can attempt a basic Reflex save with a DC of 44.

**Swarm Shape** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) The worm that walks collapses into a shapeless swarm of worms. It drops all held, worn, and carried items. While discorporated, the worm that walks can't use attack actions and can't cast spells, but it can move through areas small enough for its individual worms to fit without having to Squeeze. It can use the same action to coalesce back into its normal form.

</column>
name: The Wriggling Man

**[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2)): DC 43 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 41 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 [Barbtongued Wyvern](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2370) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 469 **Perception** +29; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +34, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +35, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +30 **Str** +9 **Dex** +6 **Con** +8 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 42 **Fort** +34 **Ref** +30 **Will** +27 **HP** 330 **Immunities** paralyzed, unconscious **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**Savage** **Trigger** A creature grabbed by the wyvern critically fails a skill check to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79). **Effect** The wyvern makes a stinger Strike against the triggering creature. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 100 feet **Melee** fangs +35 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d12+17 piercing plus 1d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Melee** barbed tongue +35 ([reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d6+17 plus Ripping Grab **Melee** stinger +35 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+17 piercing plus barbtongued wyvern venom **Pin to the Sky** With incredible force, the barbtongued wyvern whips its tongue and flings barbs from its length. Up to two creatures within 200 feet of the wyvern can be targeted; each creature must attempt a DC 40 Reflex save. The barbtongued wyvern can't use Pin to the Sky again until its next turn.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes 2d12+3 piercing damage.
**Failure** The target takes 5d12 piercing damage and is pinned in place, fastened to the underlying magical nature of Thousandbreaths by the barbs (even if not adjacent to a solid surface). It is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79). If the creature is under the effects of a spell that would prevent it from being immobilized, Thousandbreaths first attempts a [Counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check against the spell (+27).
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target takes 10d12 piercing damage and is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) 1 until it escapes. **Reel In** **Requirements** The wyvern has a creature grabbed in its tongue. **Effect** The wyvern moves the creature up to 20 feet closer to it. **Ripping Grab** **Requirements** The wyvern's last action was a success with a barbed tongue Strike, or it has a creature grabbed using this action. **Effect** If the target was immobilized by Pin to the Sky, the wyvern first painfully rips it free of the effect, dealing 4d12 piercing damage. Regardless of whether or not the target was previously immobilized, the wyvern [Grabs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) the target until the end of the monster's next turn. The wyvern can't use its tongue to Strike creatures until the Grab ends. Using Ripping Grab extends the duration of the monster's Grab until the end of its next turn for all creatures grabbed by it. A grabbed creature can use the [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) action to get out of the grab, and the Grab ends for a grabbed creature if the barbtongued wyvern moves away from it. **Barbtongued Wyvern Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** Fortitude DC 40; Maximum Duration 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 5d12 poison damage and clumsy 1 (1 round); **Stage 2** 6d12 poison damage and clumsy 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** 7d12 poison damage and clumsy 3 (1 round)
name: Barbtongued Wyvern

**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 39 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 [Defaced Naiad Queen](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2371) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 471 **Perception** +28; low-light vision **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +28, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +25, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +31, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +29, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +31, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +28, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +31, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +28, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +26 **Str** +0 **Dex** +7 **Con** +6 **Int** +4 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +8 **Shame** A defaced naiad queen suffers from shame (see below) when it attacks any target that can cast primal spells, any fey creature, or any creature that can cast divine spells and worships a deity or faith associated with nature or the First World (such as Gozreh, one of the Eldest, or the Green Faith). Forgive Foe can be attempted against a defaced naiad queen with a DC 36 Diplomacy check or Nature check. If a defaced naiad queen is affected by a critical success from Forgive Foe, its eyes are restored and it cries out in relief an instant before it vanishes, returning to the First World as a chaotic neutral naiad queen. As it does so, a wave of emotional thanks and inspiration infuse the character who used Forgive Foe, granting them a +3 status bonus to Lore checks and saves against fear effects for 24 hours. --- **AC** 38 **Fort** +27 **Ref** +30 **Will** +26 **HP** 230 **Weaknesses** cold iron 15, Resistances fire 15 **Nymph's Tragedy** (aura, emotion, enchantment, mental, primal, visual) 30 feet. Creatures that start their turn in this aura must succeed at a DC 33 Will save or become [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) as their eyes cloud with blood, darkening their vision. This effect persists as long as the creature continues to begin its turn within the aura, even if it can't see the defaced naiad on subsequent turns. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** painful caress +30 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d10+14 mental plus Tortuous Touch **Primal Prepared Spells** DC 36, attack +28 - **Cantrips (8th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=142), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=171), [Ray of Frost](/Spells.aspx?ID=245), [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=307) - **1st** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Gust of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=143), [Spider Sting](/Spells.aspx?ID=300) - **2nd** [Glitterdust](/Spells.aspx?ID=136), [Obscuring Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=210), [Web](/Spells.aspx?ID=374) - **3rd** [Blindness](/Spells.aspx?ID=26), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=289) - **4th** [Freedom of Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=128), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) (×2) - **5th** [Cone of Cold](/Spells.aspx?ID=47), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) (×2) - **6th** [Baleful Polymorph](/Spells.aspx?ID=17), [Lightning Bolt](/Spells.aspx?ID=172), [Flesh to Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=123) - **7th** [Chain Lightning](/Spells.aspx?ID=33), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=125) - **8th** [Finger of Death](/Spells.aspx?ID=116), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 36, attack +28 - **4th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) (at will) - **Constant (4th)** [Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=294) **Agonized Wail** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The defaced naiad queen unleashes an agonized wail. Each creature within 60 feet must succeed at a DC 33 Will save or become [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) 1 (or, on a critical failure, frightened 2 and [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=17) until the end of its next turn). The creature is then temporarily immune to Agonized Wail for 1 hour. **Baleful Gaze** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) When a defaced naiad queen fixes her gaze upon a foe within range of her nymph's tragedy aura, the creature must attempt a Will save. On a failure, it is affected as if by nymph's tragedy; if it was already affected by that aura, it also takes 8d6 mental damage (16d6 on a critical failure). A defaced naiad queen can use Baleful Gaze on a given creature only once per turn. **Tortuous Touch** A creature struck by the defaced naiad queen's painful caress Strike must attempt a DC 36 Fortitude save. On a failure, painful, jagged wounds open on the creature's body, the pain of which makes it [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) 1 (clumsy 2 and fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) on a critical failure). The clumsy condition is permanent until it is removed via an effect like [_restoration_](/Spells.aspx?ID=258).
The Power of Forgiveness Some of the creatures in the House at the Edge of Time—the ghostly guards and defaced naiad queens, but most notably Nyrissa herself—still harbor vestiges of goodness in their minds and souls that can be appealed to by PCs who seek to forgive them for their actions. A PC can learn about the Forgive Foe action accidentally if they seek to appeal to a creature in the House at the Edge of Time in this way on their own, or they can learn about it by closely observing how certain creatures act when they attack certain foes.
These foes have a Shame entry in their stat block. When such a foe attacks specific targets delineated in their Shame entry, they suffer a –2 status penalty on attack rolls and to all save DCs to resist their spells or attacks. Whenever such a creature takes an action that triggers this penalty, all creatures within 30 feet can attempt a Perception check against the acting creature's Deception DC. On a success, the creature attempting the check realizes the creature taking the action is suffering from guilt and shame at their own action; this reveals that a Forgive Foe action might help the acting creature recover from its plight.
name: Defaced Naiad Queen

The medusa can deactivate or activate this aura by using a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Actions.aspx?ID=683) trait.
**Biting Snakes** **Trigger** A creature ends its turn adjacent to the medusa. **Effect** The medusa makes a snake fangs Strike against the creature. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** cobra fangs +32 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d12+13 piercing plus Phomandala's venom **Melee** snake fangs +35 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 4d10+13 piercing plus Phomandala's venom **Primal Spontaneous Spells** DC 41, attack +36 - **3rd** [Blindness](/Spells.aspx?ID=26), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=147), [Meld into Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=188), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=289) (4 slots) - **4th** [Acid Arrow](/Spells.aspx?ID=2), [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34), [Freedom of Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=128), [Restoration](/Spells.aspx?ID=258), [Spell Immunity](/Spells.aspx?ID=296) (4 slots) - **7th** [Eclipse Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=96), [Finger of Death](/Spells.aspx?ID=116), [Mask of Terror](/Spells.aspx?ID=185), [Regenerate](/Spells.aspx?ID=248), [Wall of Thorns](/Spells.aspx?ID=366) (4 slots) - If the creature was already slowed by petrifying gaze before attempting its save, a failed save causes it to be [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=30) permanently. After attempting its save, the creature is then temporarily immune until the start of the medusa's next turn. **Jabberwock Bloodline** Phomandala's sorcerer bloodline is a result of Nyrissa's infusion of the medusa with jabberwock blood. This bloodline functions as the draconic bloodline, but the damage type caused is a line of sonic energy, and she uses the primal spell list instead of the arcane spell list. **Quickened Casting** **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** When Phomandala's next action is to cast a spell that is 7th level or lower, she reduces the number of actions to cast it by 1 (minimum 1 action). **Phomandala's Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** DC 41 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 6d6 poison damage and [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) 1 (1 round); **Stage 2** 9d6 poison damage and enfeebled 2 (1 round) **Woodland Stride** Even the thickest vines and roots seem to part as Phomandala moves about. Phomandala ignores difficult terrain caused by plants, such as bushes, vines, and undergrowth. Even plants manipulated by magic don't impede her progress.

**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 52 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 50 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 47 [Werendegar](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2373) **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 488 **Perception** +35; hungersense (imprecise, [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23)) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Gnomish](/Languages.aspx?ID=5), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +39, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +37, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +41, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +39, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +35, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +39, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +37 **Str** +6 **Dex** +10 **Con** +7 **Int** +8 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +10 **Hungersense** Hungersense allows the gimmerling to sense creatures that require food to live. **Items** _[+2 greater resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=147) [mithral](/Equipment.aspx?ID=275) [chainmail](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1969)_, _[+3 major striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [greater shock](/Equipment.aspx?ID=303) [wounding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=309) [rapier](/Weapons.aspx?ID=36)_ --- **AC** 47 **Fort** +36 **Ref** +39 **Will** +33 **HP** 374 **Weaknesses** cold iron 20 **Thorny Aura** ([aura](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=5), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) 15 feet. Thorny roots and barbed vines writhe and twist in the aura, creating difficult hazardous terrain that inflicts 4d6 piercing damage whenever a non-flying creature moves within the aura.
**Trip Up** **Trigger** A creature critically fails a melee attack to hit the gimmerling or moves into a space within the gimmerling's treacherous aura. **Effect** The triggering creature must attempt a DC 44 Reflex save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the start of its next turn.
**Failure** The target takes 2d10 bludgeoning damage and is flat-footed until the start of its next turn.
**Critical Failure** As failure, and the target is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).
 --- **Speed** 30 feet; trickster's step **Melee** _rapier_ +40 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 4d6+16 piercing plus 1d6 electricity and 1d6 bleed **Melee** claw +37 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 4d6+16 slashing plus Disarm **Melee** jaws +37 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 4d8+16 plus 2d8 poison **Occult Innate Spells** DC 44, attack +36 - **Cantrips (10th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334) - **7th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222), [Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=338) - **8th** [Disappearance](/Spells.aspx?ID=73), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315), [Uncontrollable Dance](/Spells.aspx?ID=347) - **9th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Telepathic Demand](/Spells.aspx?ID=336) - **10th** [Alter Reality](/Spells.aspx?ID=8) **Change Shape** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The gimmerling changes into its natural form or that of any humanoid. In humanoid form, it loses its treacherous aura, and its equipment appears to be trinkets or toys. As a humanoid that lacks claws or fangs, it loses the matching Strike. If it loses its claw Strike, it gains a fist Strike that is identical except that it deals bludgeoning damage. **Disarm** The gimmerling's last action was a success with a Strike that has Disarm in its damage entry. **Effect** The gimmerling knocks a held or carried weapon to the ground in the target's space. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** Werendegar deals 4d6 extra precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures. **Trickster's Step** The gimmerling ignores difficult terrain and doesn't trigger traps with its movement. Werendegar also avoids hazardous terrain.
name: Werendegar

markdown:
**[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 31 **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Fionn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2376) **Source** [Kingmaker Companion Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=151) pg. 18 **Perception** +24; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +27, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +27, [Fey Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +23, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +24, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +26, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +24 **Str** -5 **Dex** +5 **Con** +0 **Int** +4 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +8 **Bound to the Mortal Night** Unlike most ghosts, Fionn isn't bound to a specific site. Instead, his exile to the Material Plane has bound him to the night itself. He can travel anywhere he wishes, so long as it is night. During the day, he cannot travel further than thirty feet from the location he occupies as the sun rises. He cannot leave the Material Plane at all, and if he suffers an effect that would normally transport him to another plane, he remains on the Material Plane and instead is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) 3. --- **AC** 32 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +22 **Will** +24 **HP** 175 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), rejuvenation) **Immunities** cold, death effects, disease, paralyzed, poison, precision, unconscious **Resistances** all damage 10 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128), or Amiri's Large bastard sword **Rejuvenation** (divine, necromancy) The method for putting Fionn to rest is detailed in Putting Souls to Rest below. --- **Speed** fly 30 feet **Melee** ghostly battle axe +27 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194)), **Damage** 3d8+11 negative plus 2d6 cold **Primal Innate Spells** DC 32, attack +22 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Ray of Frost](/Spells.aspx?ID=245) - **6th** [Cone of Cold](/Spells.aspx?ID=47), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=289), [Outcast's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=211) (at will), [Talking Corpse](/Spells.aspx?ID=329) - **7th** [Eclipse Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=96), [Wall of Ice](/Spells.aspx?ID=364) **Command the Undead** ([Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** Fionn's orders are difficult for undead to ignore. When Fionn Commands the Undead, two undead allies who are lower level than him and are within 30 feet of him may Stride or Strike. **Fearsome Blizzard** ([Aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) Fionn exhales a swirling blizzard of snow and ice that chills the soul as surely as it freezes flesh. All living creatures within 60 feet of Fionn must attempt a DC 33 Fortitude save. Fionn can't use Fearsome Blizzard for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 5d8 cold damage.
**Failure** The creature takes 10d8 cold damage and is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) 1; the frightened effect has the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) traits.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 20d8 cold damage, is frightened 2, and is fleeing as long as they remain frightened. The frightened and fleeing effects have the emotion, fear, and mental traits. **Exile's Curse** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15), [Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) Fionn casts outcast's curse, extending the spell's range to 30 feet. A creature that fails its save against the spell is also [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) 1 (stupefied 2 on a critical failure) by the overwhelming sensation and belief that they have been exiled from all they hold dear. This stupefaction value cannot be reduced below 1 as long as the creature continues to be affected by [_outcast's curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=211).
name: Fionn

**[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 28 **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Xae](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2378) **Source** [Kingmaker Companion Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=151) pg. 38 **Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Gnomish](/Languages.aspx?ID=5), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Dwelling Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +22, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +21, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +22 **Str** -5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** +5 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +4 **Site Bound** --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +20 **Will** +19 **HP** 150 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), rejuvenation) **Immunities** death effects, disease, paralyzed, poison, precision, unconscious **Resistances** all damage 10 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) --- **Speed** fly 25 feet **Melee** ghostly hand +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d10+12 negative **Shyka's Judgment** ([Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) Xae clasps her hands together and cries out in Sylvan for Shyka to judge those who dare intrude upon the shrine—“Aid me, Shyka, so that your shrine will remain undefiled!” The seven statues around the perimeter of the room suddenly animate and hurl spectral versions of their hourglass-headed maces into the room, filling area A3 with a whirling vortex of pale blue-and-white energy. All living creatures in area A3 must attempt a DC 30 Will save. Xae cannot call upon Shyka's Judgment for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 6d6 mental damage.
**Failure** The creature takes 12d6 mental damage and is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) 1 as their mind struggles with memories that seem to be from past lives that weigh the character's current actions in judgment.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 18d6 mental damage and is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) 1 by their mental struggle with their judgmental past lives. The creature also takes 2d6 persistent mental damage that ends if they leave area A3. **Twist Time** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) Xae attempts a ghostly hand strike. If she hits, she inflicts damage as normal, but the target struck must also attempt a DC 30 Fortitude save (the target takes a –2 penalty to this saving throw if the strike was a critical hit).
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) 1 for 1 round as the rest of reality seems to speed up.
**Failure** The creature becomes decrepit from unnatural aging. It is slowed 2, and as long as it has the slowed condition, it is [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) 2 as well.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature feels death knocking on the door—it is doomed 1 as long as it remains slowed by Twist Time.
name: Xae
resistance_markdown: all damage 10 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103))

Unlike a normal Grapple, the annis hag doesn't need to be within reach and can move as she pleases, and a successful attempt lasts until the creature escapes (DC 25), causing the cage to crumble into rust. Any creature can attempt to destroy the cage by attacking it. It has an AC of 19, Hardness 10, and 40 Hit Points.

**Change Shape** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The hag can take on the appearance of any Medium female humanoid. This doesn't change her Speed or her attack and damage bonuses with her Strikes but might change the damage type her Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning).

**[Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=30)** <actions string="Single Action" />  claw

**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Cleansed Cultist](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2383) **Source** [Kingmaker Companion Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=151) pg. 78 **Perception** +12 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +13, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +13, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +13, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +11, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +11, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +14 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +2 **Items** [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3), [hourglass worth 10 gp](/Equipment.aspx?ID=27), [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), _+1 [shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43)_ --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +16 **Will** +14 **HP** 100 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _shortsword_ +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 1d6+5 slashing **Melee** dagger +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 1d4+5 piercing **Release the Inmost Worm** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The cleansed cultist makes fists with both hands, pressing them to their temples so that their outward pointing thumbs extend to the left and right while they cry out in painful religious rapture. The hourglass-shaped scar on their forehead bulges briefly before a bloody worm is expelled from the center of the scar. This worm shoots through the air to target a single creature within 30 feet, who must then attempt a DC 24 Fortitude save. The cultist can't Release the Inmost Worm again for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The worm lands on the target and causes agonizing pain before dropping to the floor and fading away. The target takes 3d6 mental damage.
**Failure** The worm lands on the target and burrows into their flesh, leaving no trace of a hole. The target seems to age rapidly for a moment, takes 6d6 mental damage, and is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) 1 for 1 round before the pain fades and the target returns to its actual age.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target also takes 6d6 [persistent mental damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). The target remains stupefied 1 as long as the mental damage persists, during which they appear to be aging rapidly. Once the persistent damage ends, the target's appearance reverts to normal. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** The cleansed cultist deals an extra 2d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures. **Twin Feint** The cultist makes two Strikes, one with their shortsword and one with their dagger, both against the same target. The target is automatically [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against the second attack. Apply the cultist's multiple attack penalty to the Strikes normally.
name: Cleansed Cultist

The DC of this save is equal to 27 minus the Curse Countdown's current value. **Release the Inmost Worms** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) The cleansed cultist makes fists with both hands, pressing them to their temples so that their outward pointing thumbs extend to the left and right while they cry out in painful religious rapture. **Critical Failure** As failure, but the target also takes 6d6 [persistent mental damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). The target remains stupefied 1 as long as the mental damage persists, during which they appear to be aging rapidly. Once the persistent damage ends, the target's appearance reverts to normal.

**Sound Imitation** A green hag who succeeds at a Deception check to Lie can mimic the sounds of any animal found near her lair. She has a +4 circumstance bonus to this check.As green hag.

---

**Speed** 25 feet, swim 25 feet

<actions string="Single Action" />
_scythe_ +25 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)),
**Damage** 2d10+13 slashing plus 1d6 bleed

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 2d8+13 slashing plus enfeebling humors

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 30, attack +22
- **Cantrips (6th)**
[Acid Splash](/Spells.aspx?ID=3), [Dancing Lights](/Spells.aspx?ID=58), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190)
- **2nd**
[Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (at will), [Item Facade](/Spells.aspx?ID=166), [Tree Shape](/Spells.aspx?ID=342) (at will)
- **4th**
[Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164)
- **5th**
[Illusory Scene](/Spells.aspx?ID=161), [Sending](/Spells.aspx?ID=272)
- **6th**
[Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=87)
- **Constant (6th)**
[Pass Without Trace](/Spells.aspx?ID=215), [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340), [Water Breathing](/Spells.aspx?ID=370)

**Betraying Touch** <actions string="Single Action" />  The green hag touches a creature that doesn't realize the hag is an enemy. The betrayed creature is affected by the hag's enfeebling humors and takes a –4 circumstance penalty to their saving throw against that effect.

**Change Shape** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The green hag can take on the appearance of any Medium humanoid woman. This doesn't change her Speed or her attack and damage bonuses with her Strikes, but might change the damage type her Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning).

**Enfeebling Humors**  ([Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) A creature damaged by a hag's claw must succeed at a DC 30 Fortitude save or be [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) 1 for 1 day. On a critical failure, or if it gets hit again and fails its save a second time, it's enfeebled 2 for 1 day.

**Exhale Miasma** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The green hag exhales a miasma of green vapors. Each living creature in a 15-foot cone is affected by her enfeebling humors (attempting a save as normal). She can't use Exhale Miasma again for 1d4 rounds.

**Unsettling Revelation** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** Tulvak uses Change Shape to assume her natural shape; **Effect** Tulvak's transformation into her true form is particularly revolting and can sicken those who aren't prepared for it. All creatures within 30 feet of Tulvak when she takes this reaction must attempt a DC 30 Will save or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) 1 (sickened 2 on a critical failure).

</column>

The benthic reaver is a 50-foot-tall undead monstrosity, its ribs rising like giant archways and its spine splitting into three tails. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 52 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 50 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 47 [Benthic Reaver](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2403) **Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 324 **Perception** +41; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +37, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +43, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +37 **Str** +10 **Dex** +8 **Con** +7 **Int** +6 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +6 **Comfort of the Deep** ([downtime](/Traits.aspx?ID=49)) The benthic reaver spends 1 day submerged in seawater and restores itself to full Hit Points. --- **AC** 46 [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=1) **Fort** +38 **Ref** +35 **Will** +33 **HP** 500 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** bleed, [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** physical 10 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374)) **Weaknesses** good 20 **Intense Chill** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) 30 feet. 5d6 cold damage (DC 41 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). On a failure, the creature takes a –15-foot status penalty to its Speeds for 1 round.
**Tail Sweep** **Trigger** A creature moves from beyond the reach of the benthic reaver's tail to within the reach of the benthic reaver's tail; **Effect** The benthic reaver makes a tail Strike against the triggering creature and can use [Improved Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=28) if the attack hits, even if it isn't the benthic reaver's turn. If the benthic reaver knocks the target [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31), it disrupts the triggering [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) action. --- **Speed** 30 feet, swim 60 feet **Melee** jaws +39 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d12+18 piercing plus 2d6 cold and 2d6 negative **Melee** fin +39 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d8+18 slashing plus 2d6 negative **Melee** tail +39 ([reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d10+18 slashing plus 2d6 negative and [Improved Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=28) **Ranged** eye beam +37 ([range 150 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d12+8 cold plus 4d6 negative and numbing ice **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 41 - **Constant (9th)** [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Numbing Ice** ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27)) A creature who takes damage from the benthic reaver's eye beam Strike must succeed at a DC 41 Fortitude save or become [slowed 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) as cold stiffens its limbs. A flying creature who fails its save also descends safely to the ground below. This is [forced movement](/Rules.aspx?ID=451). **Song of the Lost** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The benthic reaver unleashes a cry of mourning. All creatures within 60 feet must attempt a DC 41 Will save to resist. The benthic reaver can't use Song of the Lost for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19).
**Failure** The creature is frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature is also stunned 1 with sympathy. **Tail Sweep** The benthic reaver makes a tail Strike against each enemy within its reach. These attacks count toward the benthic reaver's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after the benthic reaver makes all its attacks.
Lurking Danger During the war between Geb and Nex, the archmage Geb created the benthic reaver from the fossilized skeleton of a leviathan. Records vividly describe the creature's haunting sounds and the cold, blue ghosts of a thousand eyes floating above its gaping maw. The unliving horror cut a swath of destruction across Nex before defenders stopped it at the nation's capital. When the defenders of Quantium drove off the besieging benthic reaver, it fled to the ocean depths off Nex's coastline to avoid destruction. Ever since, unconfirmed sightings by fishers and divers have kept its legend alive. While some see the sailors who blame the creature for unexplained shipwrecks as superstitious, mages inside Nex frequently scry the coast, afraid that a more active Geb might try to reclaim one of his greatest weapons.
name: Benthic Reaver

markdown:
<title level="1" pfs="">[Clockwork Cannoneer](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2404)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Clockwork cannoneers are cannon-armed constructs created to defend the Grand Duchy of Alkenstar. The people of Alkenstar typically find conventional arms sufficient for battling creatures who wander out of the Mana Wastes, but recent increases in activity from the fleshforges of Nex and the necromantic chambers of Geb have spurred the people of the city into a flurry of defensive innovations. Created by an experimental collaboration between the city's gunsmiths and clockwork inventors, the clockwork cannoneer provides exceptional mobile firepower. Two cannon-arms swing at the sides of the 15-foot-tall automaton, and its body contains a hopper of cannonballs that are replenished by engineers when the creature is wound up for duty. Triple-walled reinforcement on the cannoneer's black-powder reservoirs provides ample protection from the accidental explosions and backfires that often troubled early prototypes of the finely engineered machine. The current design combines the long-range capabilities of field artillery with the stopping power of a firedrake against engaged enemies, and its heat-tempered gear assembly minimizes warping that might cause slipping, grinding, or shaking.

 While reports from living artillery members serving alongside clockwork cannoneers complain of blisters, smoke inhalation, and singed eyebrows, the Grand Duchy's chief clockwork machinists have assured the city's leadership that the constructs function well within safety parameters. The clockwork cannoneer's self-sufficient construction frees crews to see to those battlefield tasks that can, for the moment, only be conducted by sentient beings.

 Intelligence reports from Quantium and Absalom indicate that attempts to replicate the clockwork cannoneer's design have been spectacularly unsuccessful.

**Bombard** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) The clockwork cannoneer launches an explosive shot up to a range of 200 feet that explodes in a 20-foot burst. Creatures within the burst take 5d10 bludgeoning damage (DC 36 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).

**Muzzleburn**   The heat from a discharged cannon sears those who touch its metal shell. The clockwork cannoneer's bore Strike deal an additional 2d6 fire damage until the end of its next turn after it makes a cannonball Strike or uses its Bombard or Powder Blast abilities.

**Powder Blast** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) The clockwork cannoneer spews flames from one of its cannons, dealing 8d6 fire damage in a 30-foot cone (DC 36 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). Those that fail the save also take 2d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Safety and Innovation</title>

Eager to solve two operational challenges with one construction feature, Alkenstar's clockwork engineers connected a gear-driven barrel cleaning mechanism to the clockwork cannoneer's winding drive. Now, operators can be sure that as long as the construct is wound, it's safe to fire. This feature is just one of the marvels of automation demonstrated by the project, along with its self-loading cannonballs and automatic powder measuring systems.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-223" />
name: Clockwork Cannoneer

markdown:
A cursed king is a grotesque mockery of ambition. When treasonous advisors, false prophets, and rebel leaders are captured by evil rulers, they're subjected to all manner of torture and humiliation before their eventual executions. But the cursed king isn't even granted dignity in death, as after their beheading, a desiccated animal head is sewn onto their neck. These horrific mummies are then dressed in fine linens and jewelry befitting royalty—a permanent warning of what happens to those who seek to rise above their station. A cursed king is bound in servitude to the ruler against whom they once fought, forced to act as a bodyguard. Even the death of their maker gives no reprieve, as they guard the true monarch's tomb forever. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Cursed King](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2405) **Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 525 **Perception** +20; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20); plus any two ancient languages (can't speak) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +23, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +17 **Str** +7 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** -4 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +20 **Ref** +20 **Will** +16 **HP** 210 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Weaknesses** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 10 **False Authority** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 30 feet. When a creature enters or ends its turn within the cursed king's aura, it must attempt a DC 26 Will save. If it fails, it becomes [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19). If it critically fails, it's cowed into submission; in addition to becoming frightened 2, it must use its first action on its turn to kneel or [flee](/Conditions.aspx?ID=17) from the cursed king. A creature who succeeds at its saving throw becomes immune to the king's false authority for 24 hours.
**Usurper's Curse** ([curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38)) When a creature slays a cursed king, it must attempt a DC 29 Will save. On a failure, it suffers the effects of the usurper's curse.
**Deadly Pursuit** **Trigger** A creature within the cursed king's reach attempts to move away; **Effect** The cursed king Strides up to its Speed, keeping the creature within reach until the creature stops or the cursed king has moved its full Speed. If the creature is still within reach, the cursed king makes a jaws Strike against it. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** jaws +23, **Damage** 2d12+13 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** fist +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d8+13 bludgeoning **Berserk** At the beginning of each of its turns in combat, a cursed king must attempt a DC 2 flat check. If it succeeds, the DC increases by 1. If it fails, it immediately goes berserk, becoming [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=108) for the rest of its turn, immune to all [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effects, and attacking the nearest creature it can reach. While it's berserk, each of its unarmed attacks gain the [deadly d8](/Actions.aspx?ID=1362) trait. At the end of a turn it failed this flat check or whenever the combat encounter ends, the DC resets to 2. **Usurper's Curse** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127)) DC 30 Will saving throw. On a failure, the creature who killed the cursed king becomes [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37). On a critical failure, it becomes stupefied 2. As long as that creature remains cursed, it can't remove or decrease the value of the stupefied condition in any way. Afflicted creatures must attempt the saving throw again every 24 hours, increasing the stupefied condition by 1 on a failure or 2 on a critical failure. If the condition ever increases past stupefied 4, they transform, body and mind, into the same animal as the cursed king's head, similar to the critical failure effect of [_baleful polymorph_](/Spells.aspx?ID=17).
Artful Punishment The cursed king's creator can take its head from any common animal in the region, but the creator typically chooses a specific head for its symbolic significance. For instance, a creator might choose a jackal's head if the cursed king was a clever tactician, a hippo's if they were formidable on the battlefield, or a giant cobra's for those who betrayed the regime from within. Aesthetics are often also considered, although it isn't unheard of for a cursed king to have the head of a more ignoble creature as an additional punishment.
name: Cursed King

<title level="1" pfs="">[Fleshforged Conformer](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2406)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Shapeshifting fleshforged conformers have adapted to survive Ecanus's high-magic, population-dense sprawl. Resembling blocky humanoid skeletons splotched in uneven layers with cartilage and muscle, conformers are capable of attaching and detaching flesh from their bodies, often sending autonomous gobbets of fat and ligament to observe living creatures and harvest biological material.

 Fleshforged conformers use their resources to augment and change their bodies, which helps them infiltrate Nexian society to better understand the arcane principles and occult secrets behind their creation. Some assume the guises of humans and animals to lead quiet, settled lives in Nex, but most are discovered and summarily exterminated as defects and threats to public safety.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 24

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/FleshforgedConformer.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 8">[Fleshforged Conformer](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2406)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="CN" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Aberration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=1" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 327

**Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); two other languages

**Skills**
[Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +15, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +17, [Fleshwarp Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +17, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +17, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +15, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +18

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +0

**Dex** +6

**Con** +3

**Int** +3

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +3
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 26 

**Fort** +17 

**Ref** +18 

**Will** +13 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 145

, regeneration 10 (deactivated by [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375))

</row>

**Weaknesses**
cold iron 10

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fanged-maw finger +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 2d10+3 piercing plus 1d8 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 25
- **Cantrips (4th)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246)
- **2nd**
[Blood Vendetta](/Spells.aspx?ID=675), [Charitable Urge](/Spells.aspx?ID=677)

**Conformance** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The conformer contorts and reshapes their body into a desired form. The conformer takes on the specific appearance of any Small, Medium, or Large [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) or [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9) that they've observed and whose appearance they remember. This doesn't change the conformer's Speed or their attack and damage bonus with their Strikes, but might change the damage type their Strikes deal.

**Surgical Detachment** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), [Morph](/Traits.aspx?ID=113), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The conformer detaches part of their body to serve them as an autonomous organism. For the next 24 hours, the conformer gains the loyal service of a [fleshforged skinskitter](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2408). As long as they remain within 1 mile of each other, the conformer can use the Surgical Detachment action again to empathically direct the actions of their skinskitter servant as well as perceive through its senses. Due to how taxing this process is, the conformer can have only one skinskitter servant at a time.

**Tissue Siphon** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) **Requirements** The conformer is within reach of an [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) or willing creature, or a creature taking [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29); **Effect** The conformer touches and absorbs some of the creature's flesh. The target must attempt a DC 26 Fortitude save; on a failure, they become [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) and take damage from their persistent bleed damage, even though it isn't the end of their turn.

**Wear Flesh** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Requirements** The conformer has used Tissue Siphon to absorb flesh within the last hour; **Effect** The conformer expends the flesh they've absorbed to transform their body, reproducing the effects of one of the following spells: [_adapt self_](/Spells.aspx?ID=594), [_enlarge_](/Spells.aspx?ID=102), [_shifting form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=535), [_tentacular limbs_](/Spells.aspx?ID=517), or [_unusual anatomy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=519). The Wear Flesh action gains the traits of the spell it's reproducing, and the conformer can Sustain a Spell on these effects.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Paranoia's Price</title>

The presence of fleshforged conformers has a predictably deleterious effect on society, even without any hostile actions taken by the fleshforged themselves. Many Nexians who know of the creatures constantly scan their neighbors for any sign of deception, and more than one reckless murder has been committed simply out of the mistaken belief they were actually a conformer is disguise. 
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-329" />
name: Fleshforged Conformer

<title level="1" pfs="">[Fleshforged Dreadnought](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2407)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Created in the arcane fleshforges of Nex, this creature was spawned as the ultimate brute-force tool for urban assaults against the cities of Geb. At 30 feet tall, the dreadnought easily tops most city walls, and Nexian infantry forces would scramble up the spikes on the creature's back to penetrate defensive fortifications. Every detail of the creature's physiology was fashioned for the battlefield, from the ballista of bone and sinew integrated into its torso to the bulky ram on the crest of its head. The fleshforged's acidic blood not only serves an important hydraulic function but also deters city defenders who might attempt to strike down the creature.

 During the war between Nex and Geb, fleshforged dreadnoughts most often appeared as part of a coordinated assault and typically replaced or augmented an army's compliment of siege weaponry. Common tactics employed by Nex's generals would direct the dreadnought to crush all opposition standing in front of a city's gate and then smash through it. Should the city's defenses prove resilient, invading troops would scale the creature to get over the city walls, relying on the dreadnought's regenerative properties to ensure it wouldn't be destroyed while helping them bypass the defensive barriers.

 Fleshforged dreadnoughts all but vanished after the disappearance of the archmage Nex, but tales of their destructive power have been preserved.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 43

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38
</column>

**Damage** 7d8+10 bludgeoning plus [Improved Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=29) 15 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />

**Damage** 4d8+18 slashing

**Damage** 4d12+8 piercing

**Destructive Charge** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The fleshforged dreadnought Strides up to twice its speed and makes a battering ram Strike against a structure or unattended object. The Strike ignores 20 points of the target's Hardness.

**Drown in Ichor** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), [Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) The fleshforged dreadnought releases a wave of caustic fluid from vents in its sides. Creatures within a 20-foot emanation take 8d6 acid damage (DC 40 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). Creatures who fail their saves also take 3d6 [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).

**[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39)** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) Huge or smaller, battering ram, DC 44

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">The Fleshforges Awaken</title>

Fleshforged dreadnoughts were integral in Nex's attacks on Geb during their long war, but most of the dangerous creatures were decommissioned after the archmage's disappearance. With Nex preparing for war, rumors of gigantic war machines rumbling over the horizon have spread through settlements bordering the Mana Wastes. Worried mayors have begun assembling evacuation plans, knowing that a dreadnought could level their towns in moments.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-329" />
name: Fleshforged Dreadnought

<title level="1" pfs="">[Fleshforged Skinskitter](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2408)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These elusive oddments resemble discarded hides of butchered animals and fleshwarping experiments, floating like so much meaty detritus in the bloody filth of Ecanus's charnel-clogged sewers. Skinskitters, named for their fluid, erratic movements, scavenge the city for leavings of fleshy waste. They prefer discreet, non-violent ways of procuring these biological materials and shy from combat, seemingly possessed of a nervous temperament. Nexian fleshforgers quip this is entirely reasonable, as skinskitters are little more than nervous systems wrapped in dying skin. Nexian guards who actually have to hunt these fleshforged pests aren't quite as jocular; confronted by the oddly childlike pleas of these cornered puddles of flesh, hair, and eyes, many recruits simply choose to leave the city watch.

 On occasion, curious scholars or soldiers have questioned why skinskitters pursue organic tissue, and whether this is a simple instinct or in pursuit of a grander purpose. Most fleshforgers assume that skinskitters consume organic matter to repair themselves in a process similar to eating, and give the matter no further thought, showing no more interest in the creatures than a smith might show in a bead of melted slag. The most paranoid Nexians claim that skinskitters are attempting to build something out of their collections of cast-off skin, perhaps even reconstructing themselves into fleshforged conformers. There is no recorded evidence of this ever occurring, however— if the creatures have any awareness at all, it's more likely that skinskitters are driven by the basest of instincts to better themselves through the same methods used in their creation but lack the understanding and self-direction to ever succeed.

**Will** +5 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 20

</row>

**Immunities**
[mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 2

**Shuddering Skitter** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** The skinskitter is reduced to 0 HP; **Effect** The fear of death agitates the skinskitter, precipitating a sudden existential awakening of consciousness. The skinskitter is reduced to 1 HP, loses the [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=108) trait and immunity to [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effects for the next 24 hours, gains the ability to speak one language (typically [Osiriani](/Languages.aspx?ID=40) or [Vudrani](/Languages.aspx?ID=45)), and takes two of the following actions in any order: [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62), [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63), Step, Stride, or [Take Cover](/Actions.aspx?ID=90). In addition, the square occupied by the skinskitter before using Shuddering Skitter becomes difficult terrain, as they disgorge a shower of half-digested calluses, chewed nails, and entangled hair in their skittering wake.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, swim 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
nail +9 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d8+1 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)

**Share the Skin** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** A [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), or willing creature is within the skinskitter's reach; **Effect** The skinskitter scrapes and shears away large sheets of the victim's skin. This requires an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the victim's Fortitude DC if the victim is grabbed and is automatic for any of the other conditions. The massive pain causes the victim to become [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) for 1 minute, and the skinskitter drapes itself in the flapping skin, granting it 10 temporary Hit Points for 1 minute.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Mindless or Not?</title>

A few truly twisted individuals have performed experiments on captive skinskitters, attempting to determine the extent of the creatures' intellectual capabilities. As skinskitters only show signs of consciousness when faced with imminent death, the research involved keeping the fleshforged dregs in constant states of mental and physical duress. Unsurprisingly, given the conditions of such research, the results of these studies are dubious at best.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-329" />
name: Skinskitter
