<title level="1" pfs="">[Soul Slime](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2426)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Soul slimes are swirling, blue-gray masses of protoplasm that feed on the quintessence contained within souls. Typically, soul slimes acquire this quintessence by consuming the living, the [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160), or souls imprisoned in other objects, although soul slimes can also feed on the quintessence of the Outer Planes and their occupants. Soul slimes digest these siphoned souls over decades. As long as a portion of a soul remains undigested, a soul slime can adopt the shape and use the vocalizations of that soul—a tactic soul slimes routinely adopt to get close to their prey while hunting. As soul slimes lack the intelligence to truly impersonate or mimic the souls they digest, they are obvious facsimiles and are entirely incapable of speech.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Ooze](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 43

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 41

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/SoulSlime.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 18">[Soul Slime](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2426)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Mindless" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=108" />
<trait label="Ooze" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=122" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #186: Ghost King's Rage](/Sources.aspx?ID=189) pg. 87

**Perception** +27; [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21) 60 feet, no vision

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +35

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +9

**Dex** -3

**Con** +6

**Int** -5

**Wis** +0

**Cha** -5
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 29 

**Fort** +33 

**Ref** +24 

**Will** +27 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 420

</row>

**Immunities**
[acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), critical hits, [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118), piercing, [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128), precision, slashing, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)

**Disgorge Soul** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** The soul slime takes physical damage; **Effect** The impact causes the soul slime to release a burst of semi-digested soul fragments. The soul slime becomes [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) until the beginning of its next turn. The soul slime can't use this concealment to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, climb 20 feet, swim 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
pseudopod +35 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d10+15 bludgeoning plus siphon soul

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
ooze globule +28 ([range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 2d10+15 bludgeoning plus siphon soul

**Disturbing Wail** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Requirements** The soul slime is using Soul Facsimile to adopt a creature's form; **Effect** The soul slime releases an incoherent wail composed of countless overlapping noises made by the creatures whose souls it has siphoned. This disturbing cacophony deals 6d10 mental damage and 6d10 sonic damage to each creature in a 30-foot-radius burst centered on the soul slime (DC 37 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save). On a failed save, a creature is additionally [stunned 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36). The soul slime can't use Disturbing Wail again for 1d4 rounds.

**[Engulf](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=14)** <actions string="Two Actions" />  DC 40, 5d10 bludgeoning, Escape DC 40, Rupture 20

**Soul Facsimile** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The soul slime assumes the shape and features of any Large, Medium, or Small creature whose soul the soul slime has siphoned for up to 1 minute. This doesn't change the soul slime's statistics, only its outward appearance. While transformed in this way, the soul slime can revert to its natural shape as a single action, and the soul slime automatically reverts to its natural shape when it takes physical damage.

**Siphon Soul**   A soul slime's touch siphons away portions of a creature's soul. Its Strikes affect [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) creatures with the effects of a [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune and deal 3d6 negative damage to living creatures and 3d6 positive damage to [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) and other creatures with [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Where In The World?</title>

Soul slimes are primarily found on the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1), feeding off the living and [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160). While they can be found anywhere living or undead gather, the most successful live on the fringes of densely populated areas, including farmlands, forests, swamps, slums, and sewers. Soul slimes also live along the River of Souls and on the Outer Planes.
</aside>
name: Soul Slime

**[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Vanth Warrior](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2427) **Source** [Pathfinder #186: Ghost King's Rage](/Sources.aspx?ID=189) pg. 18 **Perception** +25; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21) 60 feet **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19), [Requian](/Languages.aspx?ID=34) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +28, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +28, [Boneyard Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +25, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +25, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +23, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +23, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +28 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +4 **Int** +4 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 **Items** [Scythe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=39) --- **AC** 37 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +22 **Will** +28 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) **HP** 255 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) **Resistances** [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118) 10, [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) 10 **Frightful Presence** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 20 feet, DC 34.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** _scythe_ +29 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 3d12+14 slashing plus spirit touch **Melee** jaws +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 3d8+14 slashing plus spirit touch **Divine Innate Spells** DC 34, attack +26 - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (at will; self only) - **3rd** [Locate](/Spells.aspx?ID=173) (×3), [Searing Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=269) (×3) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will) - **5th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) **Infuse Weapon** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) A vanth's scythe is their symbol of office and gains a measure of their personal power. This scythe becomes a _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) scythe_ and is treated as if it were [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374) while the vanth wields it. A vanth whose scythe is taken or destroyed can infuse a new one with an hour of work. **Reaping Scythe** The vanth throws their scythe, which flies in a 60-foot line, dealing 6d8 slashing damage to all creatures in the area (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save) and returns to the vanth's hand. **Spirit Touch** A vanth's Strikes affect [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) creatures with the effects of a [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune and deal 3d6 negative damage to living creatures and 3d6 positive damage to [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160). **Vanth's Curse** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110)) **Frequency** three times per day; **Effect** The vanth bestows a curse on a creature by touching it with their scythe. The creature must attempt a DC 34 Will save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Vanth's Curse for 24 hours.
**Success** The target feels a momentary shudder of doom and is [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 minute by the distracting sensation.
**Failure** The target becomes morose and glum as it accepts its own inevitable fate. For 1 hour, the target is stupefied 3. Each time the target gains the dying condition, the stupefied value increases by 1, to a maximum value of stupefied 6.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the effect is permanent.
name: Vanth Warrior

**Overwhelming Ectoplasm**   An ectoplasmic amalgam's Strikes affect [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) creatures with the effects of a [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune. A creature damaged by an ectoplasmic amalgam's Strikes is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) unless they succeed at a DC 36 Fortitude save, as the thoughts and emotions of the amalgam's constituent souls invade their mind.

**Pummeling Barrage** <actions string="Three Actions" />  The ectoplasmic amalgam attempts a melee Strike against each enemy within the amalgam's melee reach. Each attack counts toward the amalgam's multiple attack penalty but don't increase the amalgam's penalty until it has made all its attacks.

**Spew Ectoplasm** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) The ectoplasmic amalgam releases a burst of ectoplasm that deals 16d6 bludgeoning damage in a 60-foot cone (DC 36 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). On a failed save, a creature is additionally [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) (or stunned 2 on a critical failure). Spew Ectoplasm functions as a [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75) effect for the purposes of affecting [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) creatures. The ectoplasmic amalgam can't use Spew Ectoplasm again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 46 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 44 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 41 [Seldeg Bheldis (Ghost King's Rage)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2431) **Source** [Pathfinder #186: Ghost King's Rage](/Sources.aspx?ID=189) pg. 36 **Perception** +26; darkvision **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19), [Kelish](/Languages.aspx?ID=38), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Osiriani](/Languages.aspx?ID=40) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +33, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +30, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +30, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +28, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +30, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +30 **Str** +9 **Dex** +5 **Con** +9 **Int** +6 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 **Items** [Javelin](/Weapons.aspx?ID=71) (4), [Longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=31), _+2 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [standard-grade adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=139) [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13)_, [_supreme sturdy shield_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=327) (Hardness 20^ HP 160^ BT 80) --- **AC** 41 (43 with shield raised) **Fort** +32 **Ref** +29 **Will** +26 **HP** 390 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), rejuvenation)) **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Sacrilegious Aura** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64)) 30 feet. When a creature in the aura uses a [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) spell or ability, the graveknight automatically attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) it with a +27 counteract modifier.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**Iron Command** **Trigger** An enemy within 15 feet damages Seldeg; **Effect** Seldeg puts an impertinent foe who dared harm him in their proper place. He commands the enemy to kneel in obedience. If they refuse, they must pay the price in pain and anguish. The triggering creature must choose one of the following options: the enemy kneels, dropping [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) as a free action, or the enemy refuses and takes 5d6 mental damage. In addition, Seldeg's Strikes against the triggering creature deal an additional 3 damage until the end of his next turn. Seldeg chooses whether the additional damage is evil or negative each time he uses this reaction.
**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=32)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _greater flaming longsword_ +33 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 3d8+17 slashing plus 2d10 fire **Melee** fist +34 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d8+17 bludgeoning plus 2d10 fire **Ranged** flaming javelin +30 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [thrown 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 3d6+17 piercing plus 2d10 fire **Champion Devotion Spells** DC 35, 1 Focus Point - **8th** [Touch of Corruption](/Spells.aspx?ID=819) **Devastating Blast** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) Seldeg unleashes a 30-foot cone of fire. Creatures in the area take 9d12 fire damage (DC 35 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). Seldeg can use this ability once every 1d4 rounds. **Graveknight's Curse** This curse affects anyone who wears Seldeg's armor for at least 1 hour. **Saving Throw** DC 35 Will save; **Onset** 1 hour; **Stage 1** [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9) and can't remove the armor (1 day); **Stage 2** doomed 2, hampered 10, and can't remove the armor (1 day); **Stage 3** dies and transforms into Seldeg. **Phantom Steed** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33)) Seldeg summons a supernatural mount as per [_phantom steed_](/Spells.aspx?ID=221) (8th level). Unlike _phantom steed_, the steed's AC and saving throw bonuses are all 4 levels lower than Seldeg's, and the steed has one-third of Seldeg's Hit Points (rounded down). If the steed is destroyed, Seldeg must wait 1 hour before using this ability again.
HP 130; AC 39; Fort +28, Ref +25, Will +22 **Ruinous Weapons** Any weapon or unarmed attack Seldeg uses gains the effects of the _+1 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280)_ and [_greater flaming_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295) runes. **Unyielding Block** At the start of each of Seldeg's turns, he gains two additional reactions he can use only to perform a [Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=32). **Weapon Master** Seldeg has access to the [critical specialization effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235) of any weapon he wields.
name: Seldeg Bheldis (Ghost King's Rage)

**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 41 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 [Necromancer Troop](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2434) **Source** [Pathfinder #186: Ghost King's Rage](/Sources.aspx?ID=189) pg. 40 **Perception** +35 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Kelish](/Languages.aspx?ID=38), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Osiriani](/Languages.aspx?ID=40) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +35, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +37, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +33, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +35 **Str** +10 **Dex** +5 **Con** +5 **Int** +8 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +30 **Ref** +30 **Will** +35 **HP** 270 (16 squares) **Weaknesses** area damage 15, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage 15 **[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Arcane Spontaneous Spells** DC 46, attack +38 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Ancient Dust](/Spells.aspx?ID=1261), [Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35), [Torturous Trauma](/Spells.aspx?ID=1270) - **7th** [Eclipse Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=96), [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=141), [Rouse Skeletons](/Spells.aspx?ID=988) (3 slots) - **8th** [Eclipse Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=96), [Enervation](/Spells.aspx?ID=687), [Flense](/Spells.aspx?ID=1265) (3 slots) - **9th** [Flense](/Spells.aspx?ID=1265), [Implosion](/Spells.aspx?ID=162) (2 slots) **[Form Up](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=43)** **Teach You a Lesson** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The members of the mob wildly swing their textbooks and supplies—primarily spellbooks and staves—in a chaotic attack at each enemy adjacent to the troop (DC 41 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions.
2d12+14 bludgeoning damage
3d12+14 bludgeoning damage
4d12+14 bludgeoning damage **Troop Movement** Whenever the troop Strides, they first [Form Up](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=43) as a free action to condense into a 20-foot-by-20-foot area (minus any missing squares), then moves up to their Speed. This works just like a Gargantuan creature moving; for instance, if any square of the troop enters difficult terrain, the extra movement cost applies to the whole troop. **Troop Spellcasting** When the necromancer troop Casts a Spell, their constituent members combine their efforts into casting a more powerful version of the spell than any one member could achieve alone. When Casting a Spell that has an area of a burst, cone, or line and doesn't have a duration, increase the area of that spell. Add 5 feet to the radius of a burst that normally has a radius of at least 10 feet (a burst with a smaller radius isn't affected). Add 5 feet to the length of a cone or line that's normally 15 feet long or smaller, and add 10 feet to the length of a larger cone or line.
name: Necromancer Troop

**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 48 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 46 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 43 [Vice-Chancellor Vikroti Stroh](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2435) **Source** [Pathfinder #186: Ghost King's Rage](/Sources.aspx?ID=189) pg. 42 **Perception** +33; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19), [Kelish](/Languages.aspx?ID=38), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Osiriani](/Languages.aspx?ID=40) **Skills** [Academia Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +35, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +35, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +35, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +31, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +31, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +31, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +35, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +35, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +31 **Str** +4 **Dex** +6 **Con** +5 **Int** +9 **Wis** +9 **Cha** +6 **Items** [_wand of the snowfields_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=757) (7th-level spell) --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +27 **Ref** +28 **Will** +36 **HP** 245 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), rejuvenation)) **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** physical 20 (except [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) bludgeoning) **Rejuvenation** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When Vikroti is destroyed, her soul immediately transfers to her _[soul cage](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1545)_. She can be permanently destroyed only if her soul cage is found and destroyed.
**Death's Chill** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) 15 feet. Vikroti is surrounded by an unnatural chill. Creatures that enter or begin their turn within the aura take 5d10 cold damage and are [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) (DC 37 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save).
**Counterspell** **Trigger** A creature casts a spell Vikroti has prepared; **Effect** Vikroti expends a prepared spell to counter the triggering creature's casting of that same spell. She loses her spell slot as if she'd cast the triggering spell. Vikroti then attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the triggering spell. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** hand +33 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 6d8+8 cold plus cold beyond cold **Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 44, attack +36 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Ancient Dust](/Spells.aspx?ID=1261), [Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Ray of Frost](/Spells.aspx?ID=245), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246) - **1st** [Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244), [True Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=345) (×2) - **2nd** [Blur](/Spells.aspx?ID=28), [Final Sacrifice](/Spells.aspx?ID=689), [Necromancer's Generosity](/Spells.aspx?ID=955) - **3rd** [Bind Undead](/Spells.aspx?ID=22) (×2), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=194) - **4th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=125), [Resist Energy](/Spells.aspx?ID=256) - **5th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [See Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=271), [Wall of Ice](/Spells.aspx?ID=364) - **6th** [Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=87), [False Life](/Spells.aspx?ID=108), [Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=338) - **7th** [Cone of Cold](/Spells.aspx?ID=47), [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=141), [Resist Energy](/Spells.aspx?ID=256) - **8th** [Animate Dead](/Spells.aspx?ID=666), [Polar Ray](/Spells.aspx?ID=224), [Vampiric Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=354) - **9th** [Eclipse Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=96), [Frigid Flurry](/Spells.aspx?ID=916), [Polar Ray](/Spells.aspx?ID=224) **Cold Beyond Cold** Vikroti's hand Strike deals cold damage, and the target is [slowed 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35). A successful DC 44 Fortitude save reduces this to slowed 1 (or negates on a critical success). **Drain Soul Cage** **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Vikroti taps into her _[soul cage's](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1545)_ power to cast any [arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1) spell up to the highest level she can cast, even if the spell being cast isn't one of her prepared spells. Her _soul cage_ doesn't need to be present for her to use this ability. **Steady Spellcasting** If a reaction would disrupt Vikroti's spellcasting action, Vikroti attempts a DC 15 flat check. On a success, the action isn't disrupted.
name: Vice-Chancellor Vikroti Stroh

**Source** [Pathfinder #186: Ghost King's Rage](/Sources.aspx?ID=189) pg. 44

**Perception** +30

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +28, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +32, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +28

**Ref** +30 

**Will** +22 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 365

</row>

**Immunities**
[mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)

**Controlled** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61)) Kemnebi can manipulate one of his comatose victims as if they were a puppet, maintaining complete control over each. Kemnebi's puppets are immune to all mental effects. Kemnebi can stop controlling one of his puppets as a free action. If he does, the puppet returns to its comatose state, becoming permanently [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38). Kemnebi can reassert control as a free action, causing the puppet to temporarily lose the unconscious condition.

</column>

---

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +32 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)),
**Damage** 3d6+15 bludgeoning plus [grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)

**Gang Up**   Kemnebi's puppets work together to take down more powerful foes. A puppet's Strikes deal an additional 4d6 damage against a creature that's [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by at least one of the puppet's allies.

</column>
name: Kemnebi's Puppets

[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Kelish](/Languages.aspx?ID=38), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Osiriani](/Languages.aspx?ID=40)

[Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +36, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +33, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +33, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +30, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +30

**Items**
_+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3)_, _+2 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [padded armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=3)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 39 

**Fort** +26 

**Ref** +29 

**Will** +32 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 315

</row>

**Give Them the Slip** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** Dead Faine gains the [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24), or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) condition; **Effect** Dead Faine slips out of his disguise to escape; he removes the grabbed, immobilized, and restrained conditions imposed by the triggering event, then he Steps. If Dead Faine is currently [Impersonating](/Actions.aspx?ID=46), his disguise is ruined, and he's exposed as himself.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_dagger_ +33 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 3d4+8 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +33 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d4+8 bludgeoning

**Innate Occult Spells** DC 35
- **2nd**
[Mimic Undead](/Spells.aspx?ID=948) (×2)
- **3rd**
[Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=159)
- **5th**
[Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340)

**Adopt Persona** <actions string="Three Actions" />  Dead Faine attempts a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) check to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46). He doesn't take a penalty to this check due to lacking a [disguise kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=19), to appear as an [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160), or to appear as a specific individual.

**Feinting Strike** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Frequency** once per round; **Requirements** Dead Faine is within melee reach of an enemy; **Effect** Dead Faine [Feints](/Actions.aspx?ID=48), then Strikes the target of his Feint.

**[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)**   Dead Faine deals an additional 4d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures. This increases to 6d6 against creatures flat-footed due to Dead Faine's Feint or Traitorous Betrayal.

**Traitorous Betrayal** <actions string="Two Actions" />  Dead Faine Interacts to draw his dagger and Strikes with it. If the target believes Dead Faine was anyone other than himself, the target is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against Dead Faine's attack.

</column>
name: Dead Faine

markdown:
Long ago, in the days before Geb was a nation, Kemnebi was a mortal man with a wife and family. Tragedy struck, and the kindly father dove into dark magic to save the lives of those he loved. Kemnebi can't recall what events he took part in during those early days; he'd long since purged the memories from his mind to make room for memories he considered more useful as well as a wealth of knowledge and skills. What he does recall is by the end of his mortal life his family was dead, he was alone, and he died, coming back through spite, determination, and [Urgathoa's](/Deities.aspx?ID=19) blessing as a [vetalarana](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=311). Kemnebi was reckless in his early days and was nearly destroyed by the monk Balji, whose death he drew out over years as an act of vengeance. Eventually, Balji died, but the simple monk would live on inside Kemnebi, who had let in too many of the monk's memories. This new, enlightened Kemnebi was a far more cautious, cunning, and terrifying predator. He has destroyed countless lives, fed off millions, and rose through Geb's hierarchy to become the most powerful Blood Lord within the nation. With [Arazni's](/Deities.aspx?ID=31) departure from Geb, the ghost king has returned to rule his nation, and Kemnebi has been forced to accelerate his plans—plans that would've devastated Geb's trade partners, sparked a war, and seen Kemnebi rival Geb himself. Now that the PCs have foiled Kemnebi's plans, Kemnebi has retreated into hiding in his shadow sanctum. Campaign Role Kemnebi is chief among the Blood Lords and the main villain of the Blood Lords Adventure Path. While the characters spend most of the campaign foiling Kemnebi's plans and saving their nation from war, in “Ghost King's Rage,” the characters must finally find and destroy Kemnebi, who hides within his shadow sanctum and plots revenge against both Geb and the meddlesome Blood Lords who have foiled centuries of planning and preparation. Kemnebi is unrepentant and difficult to destroy. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 52 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 50 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 47 [Chancellor Kemnebi](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2441) **Source** [Pathfinder #186: Ghost King's Rage](/Sources.aspx?ID=189) pg. 88 **Perception** +39; [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +35, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +43, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +33, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +41, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +36, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +36, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +36, [Mechitar Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +41, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +41, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +41, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +37, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +43, [Shadow Plane Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +41, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +41, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +35 **Str** +6 **Dex** +8 **Con** +7 **Int** +10 **Wis** +10 **Cha** +8 **Thoughtsense** ([occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) Kemnebi senses all non-[mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=108) creatures within 100 feet as a [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) sense. **Items** [religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=41) of [Urgathoa](/Deities.aspx?ID=19), [ring of spell turning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=458), [wand of widening](/Equipment.aspx?ID=373) (9th level) --- **AC** 43 **Fort** +34 **Ref** +35 **Will** +41 **HP** 350 ([fast healing 20](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15), mental rebirth, [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Resistances** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) 15, physical 15 (except [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375)) **Weaknesses** vetalarana vulnerabilities **Enlightened Mind** Whenever Kemnebi is targeted by a mental effect, that effect's source or caster must succeed at a DC 5 flat check. If this check fails, the mental effect doesn't affect Kemnebi.
**Mental Rebirth curse, incapacitation, mental, necromancy, occult)** Unlike most other undead, a vetalarana isn't destroyed when they reach 0 HP. Instead, their body is destroyed, crumbling to ash, and their mind is immediately transferred into a creature they rendered comatose through Drain Thoughts. This creature must be within 1 mile; if no such creature is in range, the vetalarana is destroyed. The creature becomes [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6) by the vetalarana and loses the [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) and [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) conditions. If the creature controlled by a vetalarana in this way is killed, the vetalarana's mind transfers to another creature within 1 mile who they rendered comatose through Drain Thoughts.
After 1d6 days of being controlled, the controlled creature dies, and its body transforms into that of the vetalarana who rendered it comatose.
**Vetalarana Vulnerabilities** All vetalaranas possess the following weaknesses.
  • **Barred Senses** A vetalarana can't see or sense through [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75) effects or [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375), and their mind can't penetrate force effects or cold iron. Such barriers can prevent the transference of a vetalarana's mind through Mental Rebirth.
  • **Revulsion** A vetalarana delights in consuming memories and secrets, and is disgusted by those who share such sensitive tidbits openly. A vetalarana can't voluntarily come within 10 feet of a creature loudly voicing their intimate personal secrets or most cherished memories aloud. Speaking such information aloud with conviction is a single action that has the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), and [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101) traits, and protects the creature until the start of its next turn. A vetalarana that involuntarily comes within 10 feet of a creature sharing such information gains the fleeing condition until they're 10 feet or farther from the creature. After 1 round of being exposed to the secrets of a creature, a vetalarana can attempt a DC 25 Will save as a single action, which has the concentrate trait. On a success, they overcome their revulsions for 1d6 rounds (or 1 hour on a critical success).
  • **Vulnerable to Brain Destruction** A vetalarana whose brain has been destroyed, or is reduced to 0 HP while unable to take [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) actions, can't use Mental Rebirth.

**Deflect Attack** **Requirements** Kemnebi is aware of the attack, isn't [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16), and has a hand free; **Trigger** Kemnebi is the target of a physical ranged attack or ranged spell attack; **Effect** Kemnebi gains a +4 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. If the attack misses, Kemnebi has deflected it. He can't use this reaction to deflect unusually massive ranged projectiles (such as boulders or ballista bolts). --- **Speed** 40 feet, climb 40 feet **Melee** claw +39 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 4d10+16 slashing plus paralyzing claws and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 48, attack +40 - **10th** [Flense](/Spells.aspx?ID=1265) **Divine Prepared Spells** DC 48, attack +40 - **Cantrips (10th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=84), [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=307), [Torturous Trauma](/Spells.aspx?ID=1270) - **5th** [Blink Charge](/Spells.aspx?ID=872), [Blood Vendetta](/Spells.aspx?ID=675), [Crushing Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=57) - **6th** [Blade Barrier](/Spells.aspx?ID=24), [Chilling Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=36), [Crisis of Faith](/Spells.aspx?ID=55) - **10th** [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) (×9), [Spirit Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=301) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 48 - **Constant (5th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Focus Spells** DC 48, attack +40, 3 Focus Points - **10th** [Athletic Rush](/Spells.aspx?ID=402), [Call of the Grave](/Spells.aspx?ID=522), [Enduring Might](/Spells.aspx?ID=419), [Life Siphon](/Spells.aspx?ID=532) **Rituals** DC 48 - **2nd** [Create Undead](/Rituals.aspx?ID=10) (doesn't require secondary casters) **Center Self** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** Kemnebi regains 1 Focus Point. If he's [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37), he reduces the value of his stupefied condition by one. If Kemnebi is affected by a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effect or spell, Kemnebi attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) that effect or spell with a +33 counteract modifier. **Control Comatose** ([Exploration](/Traits.aspx?ID=66), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Possession](/Traits.aspx?ID=129)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The vetalarana manipulator spends 10 minutes to take psychic control of the creatures they've rendered comatose with Drain Thoughts. This has the effects of the [_possession_](/Spells.aspx?ID=225) spell, but lasts for up to 6 hours, has a range of 1 mile, and the vetalarana can control a number of comatose victims at a time equal to half the vetalarana's level. A comatose victim can't resist this possession and automatically gets a critical failure on its saving throw against Control Comatose.
As long as a comatose victim is [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6) by a vetalarana manipulator in this way, the victim's [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) and [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) conditions are temporarily suppressed. **Drain Thoughts** ([Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Requirements** A [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), or willing creature is within Kemnebi's reach; **Effect** Kemnebi seizes a creature and consumes its memories. This requires an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the victim's Fortitude DC if the victim is grabbed and is automatic for any of the other conditions. The victim is [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37)f and Kemnebi regains 35 HP, gaining any excess HP as temporary Hit Points. Draining Thoughts from a creature that's already stupefied doesn't restore any HP to Kemnebi but increases the victim's stupefied value by 2. Additionally, Kemnebi views one of the victim's memories and can choose to alter, enhance, or erase the memory he views as 4th-level [_modify memory_](/Spells.aspx?ID=200).
A victim's stupefied condition value decreases by 1 every week.
A victim who becomes stupefied 5 in this way is rendered comatose, becoming permanently unconscious. As long as Kemnebi lives, a comatose victim can't regain consciousness, and its stupefied condition doesn't decrease. **Flurry of Blows** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** Kemnebi makes two claw Strikes. If both hit the same creature, combine their damage for the purposes of resistances and weaknesses. Kemnebi applies his multiple attack penalty to the Strikes normally. **Paralyzing Claws** ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) Any living, non-[mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=108) creature hit by Kemnebi's claw Strike must succeed at a DC 36 Fortitude save or become [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28). It can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns, and the DC cumulatively decreases by 1 on each such save.
name: Chancellor Kemnebi
